{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sarashina Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Sarashina-jinja 1.jpg|300px]] | location = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, {{ill|Chikuma|ja|千曲市}}, Wakamiya 2 | coordinates = {{Coord|36|29|14.7|N|138|8|31.08|E|scale:30000}} | deities = {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto|ja|誉田別尊}}
{{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|気長足姫尊}}
{{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|大鷦鷯命}} | founded = | main_hall= | other_name = Wakamiya Hachiman | main_festival = September 23 | events = }} '''Sarashina Shrine''' (さらしなじんじゃ) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Chikuma|ja|千曲市}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}. It is a {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}, and under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it held the status of a ''gōsha'' (village shrine). == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto|ja|誉田別尊}} * {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|気長足姫尊}} * {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|大鷦鷯命}} == History == According to shrine tradition, it was initially enshrined by {{ill|Prince Sakaibe no Kurohiko|ja|坂合黒彦皇子}} on a ridge of the Sarashina mountains. However, following a landslide caused by an earthquake in the third year of the {{ill|Ninna era|ja|仁和}} (887 CE), the shrine was relocated to the mountain's base. The shrine is referred to as "Wakamiya" in the {{ill|Heike Monogatari|ja|平家物語}}, specifically in relation to an event in the first year of the {{ill|Yōwa era|ja|養和}} when {{ill|Kiso Yoshinaka|ja|木曽義仲}} reportedly "prostrated himself before Hachiman Shrine." It was known as Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine until the {{ill|Hōreki era|ja|宝暦}} (1757 CE), when it assumed its current name. By 1759, records of the {{ill|Matsushiro Domain|ja|松代藩}} noted the shrine’s landholdings at three ''koku''. Before the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離令}} (separation of Shinto and Buddhism), the shrine was associated with a {{ill|Shingon sect temple|ja|真言宗}} known as Wakamiya Hachiman Jingū-ji. File:Sarashina-jinja torii.jpg|Torii File:Sarashina-jinja 2.jpg|Main shrine building File:Sarashina-jinja cenotaph.jpg|{{ill|Cenotaph for loyal souls|ja|忠魂碑}} * '''Treasure Tower (Hōkyōintō):''' The grounds contain an ancient stone pagoda inscribed with the year {{ill|Eiwa era|ja|永和}} 2 (1378 CE). It is believed to mark the burial site of 45 local warriors who pledged their loyalty to Prince {{ill|Morinaga|ja|宗良親王}} and prepared for death before heading to battle. It was designated a tangible cultural property of the city in 1988. * '''Earthworks:''' The northern and eastern sides of the shrine grounds retain traces of medieval fortifications, possibly linked to a {{ill|Southern Court (Japan)|ja|南朝}} loyalist stronghold. * '''Slender Bronze Sword:''' Discovered during the Edo period in the nearby {{ill|Yazuka Ruins|ja|箭塚遺跡}}, this sword became a sacred treasure of the shrine. It drew attention from historians for demonstrating that bronze cultures from {{ill|Northern Kyushu|ja|北九州}} extended far beyond the {{ill|Kansai region|ja|近畿圏}}. It was designated a tangible cultural property of the city in 1988. * '''Torii Gate:''' The current torii faces east towards the {{ill|Chikuma River|ja|千曲川}}, which historically marked the rear of the shrine. The traditional approach was from the west, via the mountains and the ancient Hachioji-san Manji Pass. The shrine once served the worship needs of the three former villages of Wakamiya, Kurohiko, and Shibahara. Of these, Kurohiko Village, with a population of 1,000 non-agricultural households, was reportedly devastated and dispersed by frequent floods during the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代}}. The names of Kurohiko and Wakamiya are thought to derive from the deeds of the two princes linked to the shrine's origins. == References == == Bibliography == * ''Shrines and Temples in the Shinano Cultural Series,'' 1981, {{ill|Shinano Mainichi Shimbun|ja|信濃毎日新聞社}}. * ''{{ill|Kadokawa Japan Place names Dictionary|ja|角川日本地名大辞典}} Vol. 20: Nagano Prefecture,'' 1990, {{ill|Kadokawa Shoten|ja|角川書店}}. {{Commonscat}} {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=Nagano Prefecture}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Sarashina Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gōsha shrines]] [[Category:Shikinai shrines in Shinano Province]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Chikuma, Nagano]] [[Category:History of Chikuma, Nagano]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[sn:Sarashina Shrine]] [[ja:佐良志奈神社]] [[en:Sarashina Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Sarashina Shrine}}

{{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|大鷦鷯命|12=draft}} | founded = | main_hall= | other_name = Wakamiya Hachiman | main_festival = September 23 | events = }} '''Sarashina Shrine''' (さらしなじんじゃ) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Chikuma|ja|千曲市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|12=draft}}. It is a {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社|12=draft}} listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|12=draft}}, and under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=draft}}, it held the status of a ''gōsha'' (village shrine). == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto|ja|誉田別尊|12=draft}} * {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|気長足姫尊|12=draft}} * {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|大鷦鷯命|12=draft}} == History == According to shrine tradition, it was initially enshrined by {{ill|Prince Sakaibe no Kurohiko|ja|坂合黒彦皇子|12=draft}} on a ridge of the Sarashina mountains. However, following a landslide caused by an earthquake in the third year of the {{ill|Ninna era|ja|仁和|12=draft}} (887 CE), the shrine was relocated to the mountain's base. The shrine is referred to as "Wakamiya" in the {{ill|Heike Monogatari|ja|平家物語|12=draft}}, specifically in relation to an event in the first year of the {{ill|Yōwa era|ja|養和|12=draft}} when {{ill|Kiso Yoshinaka|ja|木曽義仲|12=draft}} reportedly "prostrated himself before Hachiman Shrine." It was known as Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine until the {{ill|Hōreki era|ja|宝暦|12=draft}} (1757 CE), when it assumed its current name. By 1759, records of the {{ill|Matsushiro Domain|ja|松代藩|12=draft}} noted the shrine’s landholdings at three ''koku''. Before the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離令|12=draft}} (separation of Shinto and Buddhism), the shrine was associated with a {{ill|Shingon sect temple|ja|真言宗|12=draft}} known as Wakamiya Hachiman Jingū-ji. File:Sarashina-jinja torii.jpg|Torii File:Sarashina-jinja 2.jpg|Main shrine building File:Sarashina-jinja cenotaph.jpg|{{ill|Cenotaph for loyal souls|ja|忠魂碑|12=draft}} * '''Treasure Tower (Hōkyōintō):''' The grounds contain an ancient stone pagoda inscribed with the year {{ill|Eiwa era|ja|永和|12=draft}} 2 (1378 CE). It is believed to mark the burial site of 45 local warriors who pledged their loyalty to Prince {{ill|Morinaga|ja|宗良親王|12=draft}} and prepared for death before heading to battle. It was designated a tangible cultural property of the city in 1988. * '''Earthworks:''' The northern and eastern sides of the shrine grounds retain traces of medieval fortifications, possibly linked to a {{ill|Southern Court (Japan)|ja|南朝|12=draft}} loyalist stronghold. * '''Slender Bronze Sword:''' Discovered during the Edo period in the nearby {{ill|Yazuka Ruins|ja|箭塚遺跡|12=draft}}, this sword became a sacred treasure of the shrine. It drew attention from historians for demonstrating that bronze cultures from {{ill|Northern Kyushu|ja|北九州|12=draft}} extended far beyond the {{ill|Kansai region|ja|近畿圏|12=draft}}. It was designated a tangible cultural property of the city in 1988. * '''Torii Gate:''' The current torii faces east towards the {{ill|Chikuma River|ja|千曲川|12=draft}}, which historically marked the rear of the shrine. The traditional approach was from the west, via the mountains and the ancient Hachioji-san Manji Pass. The shrine once served the worship needs of the three former villages of Wakamiya, Kurohiko, and Shibahara. Of these, Kurohiko Village, with a population of 1,000 non-agricultural households, was reportedly devastated and dispersed by frequent floods during the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|12=draft}}. The names of Kurohiko and Wakamiya are thought to derive from the deeds of the two princes linked to the shrine's origins. == References == == Bibliography == * ''Shrines and Temples in the Shinano Cultural Series,'' 1981, {{ill|Shinano Mainichi Shimbun|ja|信濃毎日新聞社|12=draft}}. * ''{{ill|Kadokawa Japan Place names Dictionary|ja|角川日本地名大辞典|12=draft}} Vol. 20: Nagano Prefecture,'' 1990, {{ill|Kadokawa Shoten|ja|角川書店|12=draft}}. {{Commonscat}} {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=Nagano Prefecture}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Sarashina Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gōsha shrines]] [[Category:Shikinai shrines in Shinano Province]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Chikuma, Nagano]] [[Category:History of Chikuma, Nagano]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[sn:Sarashina Shrine]] [[ja:佐良志奈神社]] [[en:Sarashina Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Sarashina Shrine}}

{{神社 |名称 = 佐良志奈神社 |画像 = [[File:Sarashina-jinja 1.jpg|300px]] |所在地 = [[長野県]][[千曲市]]若宮2番地 |位置= {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|29|14.7|N|138|8|31.08|E|scale:30000}} |祭神 = [[応神天皇|誉田別尊]]
[[神功皇后|気長足姫尊]]
[[仁徳天皇|大鷦鷯命]]　 |社格 = |創建 = |本殿 = |別名 = 若宮八幡 |札所等 = |例祭 = 9月23日 |神事 = }} '''佐良志奈神社'''（さらしなじんじゃ）は[[長野県]][[千曲市]]にある神社。『[[延喜式神名帳]]』に記載される[[式内社]]であり、[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は郷社。 == 祭神 == *[[応神天皇|誉田別尊]] *[[神功皇后|気長足姫尊]] *[[仁徳天皇|大鷦鷯命]] == 歴史 == 社伝によれば、当初は[[坂合黒彦皇子]]が勧請し、更級山地の支嶺に鎮座していたが、[[仁和]]3年（[[887年]]）の震災により社地が崩落し、山麓に遷座したとされる。 『[[源平盛衰記]]』（[[養和]]元年に[[源義仲|木曽義仲]]が「八幡社を伏拝み」とあるのは当社である事を否定できない）には「若宮」とあり「信州の文化シリーズ 寺と神社」p196、中世まで若宮八幡宮と称した。 [[宝暦]]7年（[[1757年]]）に現在の社名となった。同9年（[[1759年]]）の[[松代藩]]書上では社領3石であった「角川日本地名大辞典」 p.534。 また神仏分離令以前は[[真言宗|別当寺]]として[[真言宗]]の若宮八幡[[神宮寺]]があった。 File:Sarashina-jinja torii.jpg|鳥居 File:Sarashina-jinja 2.jpg|社殿 File:Sarashina-jinja cenotaph.jpg|[[忠魂碑]] *[[宝篋印塔]] - 境内には[[永和 (日本)|永和]]2年（1378年）銘の古い石塔が残されている。[[後醍醐天皇]]の皇子[[宗良親王]]に決死の従軍を誓った在地の武士４５名が戦地に赴く前に自分達の墓とした逆襲塚とされる。昭和６３年に市の有形文化財に指定されている。 *土塁跡 - 中世の城館跡を思わせる土塁跡が境内の北側と東側にある。[[南朝 (日本)|南朝]]方武士団の拠点であった名残りかも知れない。 *細形銅剣 - [[江戸時代]]の文化年間に近くの箭塚遺跡から発掘され、この神社の社宝とされた。[[昭和]]63年（1988年）に市の[[有形文化財]]に指定された。北九州が中心とされていた銅器文化が[[近畿圏]]を遥かに超えた当地で出土していたことについて歴史学会から注目されていた。 *かつて存在していた若宮村・黒彦村・芝原村の3か村で氏子を構成していたとされる。この内の旧黒彦村については1,000軒もの非農耕者の大きな村落であったが[[戦国時代 (日本)|戦国時代]]に度々の大洪水で流亡分散したと伝えられる。この黒彦と若宮の地名については神社の由来に関係する2人の皇子の事績に因むものと考えられている。 *鳥居 - 現在は東側の[[千曲川]]に面して建てられているが、かつてそこは神社の裏側に当たっていた。古くからの[[戸倉宿]]と[[明治]]になって開湯した[[上山田温泉]]とを結んぶ渡し舟はあったが[[戸倉駅]]の開設によって[[大正橋 (千曲市)|大正橋]]が架橋されて表側とされるようになった。本来の[[参道]]は西方の山側から入っていて古来は八王子山万治峠越えをする道からの参詣であった。 *洞窟 - 現在は塞がれているが境内南側から抜けて上山田側に通じる[[地下通路]]があった。[[第二次大戦]]中に洞窟が掘られ[[軍需工場]]を設け一応の完成を見たが、その翌日に終戦となった[[戦争遺跡]]と言われる。以後[[稲荷社]]が祀られたり、飲酒提供の店が営まれた時もあったが戦後２０年程で閉ざされたと伝わる。 == 脚注 == == 参考文献 == *『信州の文化シリーズ 寺と神社』 1981年 [[信濃毎日新聞|信濃毎日新聞社]] *『[[角川日本地名大辞典]] 20 長野県』 1990年 [[角川書店]] {{Commonscat}} {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=長野県}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:さらしなしんしや}} [[Category:長野県の神社]] [[Category:長野県の旧郷社]] [[Category:信濃国の式内社]] [[Category:千曲市の建築物]] [[Category:千曲市の歴史]]

{{神社 |名称 = 佐良志奈神社 |画像 = [[File:Sarashina-jinja 1.jpg|300px]] |所在地 = [[長野県]][[千曲市]]若宮2番地 |位置= {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|29|14.7|N|138|8|31.08|E|scale:30000}} |祭神 = [[応神天皇|誉田別尊]]
[[神功皇后|気長足姫尊]]
[[仁徳天皇|大鷦鷯命]]　 |社格 = |創建 = |本殿 = |別名 = 若宮八幡 |札所等 = |例祭 = 9月23日 |神事 = }} '''佐良志奈神社'''（さらしなじんじゃ）は[[長野県]][[千曲市]]にある神社。『[[延喜式神名帳]]』に記載される[[式内社]]であり、[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は郷社。 == 祭神 == *[[応神天皇|誉田別尊]] *[[神功皇后|気長足姫尊]] *[[仁徳天皇|大鷦鷯命]] == 歴史 == 社伝によれば、当初は[[坂合黒彦皇子]]が勧請し、更級山地の支嶺に鎮座していたが、[[仁和]]3年（[[887年]]）の震災により社地が崩落し、山麓に遷座したとされる。 『[[源平盛衰記]]』（[[養和]]元年に[[源義仲|木曽義仲]]が「八幡社を伏拝み」とあるのは当社である事を否定できない）には「若宮」とあり「信州の文化シリーズ 寺と神社」p196、中世まで若宮八幡宮と称した。 [[宝暦]]7年（[[1757年]]）に現在の社名となった。同9年（[[1759年]]）の[[松代藩]]書上では社領3石であった「角川日本地名大辞典」 p.534。 また神仏分離令以前は[[真言宗|別当寺]]として[[真言宗]]の若宮八幡[[神宮寺]]があった。 File:Sarashina-jinja torii.jpg|鳥居 File:Sarashina-jinja 2.jpg|社殿 File:Sarashina-jinja cenotaph.jpg|[[忠魂碑]] *[[宝篋印塔]] - 境内には[[永和 (日本)|永和]]2年（1378年）銘の古い石塔が残されている。[[後醍醐天皇]]の皇子[[宗良親王]]に決死の従軍を誓った在地の武士４５名が戦地に赴く前に自分達の墓とした逆襲塚とされる。昭和６３年に市の有形文化財に指定されている。 *土塁跡 - 中世の城館跡を思わせる土塁跡が境内の北側と東側にある。[[南朝 (日本)|南朝]]方武士団の拠点であった名残りかも知れない。 *細形銅剣 - [[江戸時代]]の文化年間に近くの箭塚遺跡から発掘され、この神社の社宝とされた。[[昭和]]63年（1988年）に市の[[有形文化財]]に指定された。北九州が中心とされていた銅器文化が[[近畿圏]]を遥かに超えた当地で出土していたことについて歴史学会から注目されていた。 *かつて存在していた若宮村・黒彦村・芝原村の3か村で氏子を構成していたとされる。この内の旧黒彦村については1,000軒もの非農耕者の大きな村落であったが[[戦国時代 (日本)|戦国時代]]に度々の大洪水で流亡分散したと伝えられる。この黒彦と若宮の地名については神社の由来に関係する2人の皇子の事績に因むものと考えられている。 *鳥居 - 現在は東側の[[千曲川]]に面して建てられているが、かつてそこは神社の裏側に当たっていた。古くからの[[戸倉宿]]と[[明治]]になって開湯した[[上山田温泉]]とを結んぶ渡し舟はあったが[[戸倉駅]]の開設によって[[大正橋 (千曲市)|大正橋]]が架橋されて表側とされるようになった。本来の[[参道]]は西方の山側から入っていて古来は八王子山万治峠越えをする道からの参詣であった。 *洞窟 - 現在は塞がれているが境内南側から抜けて上山田側の明治新道に通じる[[地下通路]]があった。[[第二次大戦]]中に洞窟が掘られ[[軍需工場]]を設けて一応の完成を見たが、その翌日に終戦となった[[戦争遺跡]]と言われる。以後洞窟内に[[稲荷社]]が祀られたり、飲酒提供の店が営まれた時もあったが戦後２０年程で閉ざされたと伝わる。 *カタクリ祭り - 境内南面の北向き斜面にカタクリの群生地がある。３月末から４月の第１週が花の見頃でカタクリ祭りが設定され親しまれている。 == 脚注 == == 参考文献 == *『信州の文化シリーズ 寺と神社』 1981年 [[信濃毎日新聞|信濃毎日新聞社]] *『[[角川日本地名大辞典]] 20 長野県』 1990年 [[角川書店]] {{Commonscat}} {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=長野県}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:さらしなしんしや}} [[Category:長野県の神社]] [[Category:長野県の旧郷社]] [[Category:信濃国の式内社]] [[Category:千曲市の建築物]] [[Category:千曲市の歴史]]

{{神社 |名称 = 佐良志奈神社 |画像 = [[File:Sarashina-jinja 1.jpg|300px]] |所在地 = [[長野県]][[千曲市]]若宮2番地 |位置= {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|29|14.7|N|138|8|31.08|E|scale:30000}} |祭神 = [[応神天皇|誉田別尊]]
[[神功皇后|気長足姫尊]]
[[仁徳天皇|大鷦鷯命]]　 |社格 = |創建 = |本殿 = |別名 = 若宮八幡 |札所等 = |例祭 = 9月23日 |神事 = }} '''佐良志奈神社'''（さらしなじんじゃ）は[[長野県]][[千曲市]]にある神社。『[[延喜式神名帳]]』に記載される[[式内社]]であり、[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は郷社。 == 祭神 == *[[応神天皇|誉田別尊]] *[[神功皇后|気長足姫尊]] *[[仁徳天皇|大鷦鷯命]] == 歴史 == 社伝によれば、当初は[[坂合黒彦皇子]]が勧請し、更級山地の支嶺に鎮座していたが、[[仁和]]3年（[[887年]]）の震災により社地が崩落し、山麓に遷座したとされる。 『[[源平盛衰記]]』（[[養和]]元年に[[源義仲|木曽義仲]]が「八幡社を伏拝み」とあるのは当社である事を否定できない）には「若宮」とあり「信州の文化シリーズ 寺と神社」p196、中世まで若宮八幡宮と称した。 [[宝暦]]7年（[[1757年]]）に現在の社名となった。同9年（[[1759年]]）の[[松代藩]]書上では社領3石であった「角川日本地名大辞典」 p.534。 また神仏分離令以前は[[真言宗|別当寺]]として[[真言宗]]の若宮八幡[[神宮寺]]があった。 File:Sarashina-jinja torii.jpg|鳥居 File:Sarashina-jinja 2.jpg|社殿 File:Sarashina-jinja cenotaph.jpg|[[忠魂碑]] *[[宝篋印塔]] - 境内には[[永和 (日本)|永和]]2年（1378年）銘の古い石塔が残されている。[[後醍醐天皇]]の皇子[[宗良親王]]に決死の従軍を誓った在地の武士４５名が戦地に赴く前に自分達の墓とした逆襲塚とされる。昭和６３年に市の有形文化財に指定されている。 *土塁跡 - 中世の城館跡を思わせる土塁跡が境内の北側と東側にある。[[南朝 (日本)|南朝]]方武士団の拠点であった名残りかも知れない。 *細形銅剣 - [[江戸時代]]の文化年間に近くの箭塚遺跡から発掘され、この神社の社宝とされた。[[昭和]]63年（1988年）に市の[[有形文化財]]に指定された。北九州が中心とされていた銅器文化が[[近畿圏]]を遥かに超えた当地で出土していたことについて歴史学会から注目されていた。 *かつて存在していた若宮村・黒彦村・芝原村の3か村で氏子を構成していたとされる。この内の旧黒彦村については1,000軒もの非農耕者の大きな村落であったが[[戦国時代 (日本)|戦国時代]]に度々の大洪水で流亡分散したと伝えられる。この黒彦と若宮の地名については神社の由来に関係する2人の皇子の事績に因むものと考えられている。 *鳥居 - 現在は東側の[[千曲川]]に面して建てられているが、かつてそこは神社の裏側に当たっていた。古くからの[[戸倉宿]]と[[明治]]になって開湯した[[上山田温泉]]とを結んぶ渡し舟はあったが[[戸倉駅]]の開設によって[[大正橋 (千曲市)|大正橋]]が架橋されて表側とされるようになった。本来の[[参道]]は西方の山側から入っていて古来は八王子山万治峠越えをする道からの参詣であった。 *洞窟 - 現在は塞がれているが境内南側から抜けて上山田側の明治新道に通じる[[地下通路]]があった。[[第二次大戦]]中に洞窟が掘られ[[軍需工場]]を設けて一応の完成を見たが、その翌日に終戦となった[[戦争遺跡]]と言われる。以後洞窟内に[[稲荷社]]が祀られたり、飲酒提供の店が営まれた時もあったが戦後２０年程で閉ざされたと伝わる。 *カタクリ祭り - 境内南面の北向き斜面に[[カタクリ]]の[[群生地]]があり「みどりのサポート隊」が管理し観察会を続ける。３月末から４月の第１週辺りが花の見頃でカタクリ祭りが設定され親しまれている。 == 脚注 == == 参考文献 == *『信州の文化シリーズ 寺と神社』 1981年 [[信濃毎日新聞|信濃毎日新聞社]] *『[[角川日本地名大辞典]] 20 長野県』 1990年 [[角川書店]] {{Commonscat}} {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=長野県}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:さらしなしんしや}} [[Category:長野県の神社]] [[Category:長野県の旧郷社]] [[Category:信濃国の式内社]] [[Category:千曲市の建築物]] [[Category:千曲市の歴史]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sarashina Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Sarashina-jinja 1.jpg|300px]] | location = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|12=draft}}, {{ill|Chikuma|ja|千曲市|12=draft}}, Wakamiya 2 | coordinates = {{Coord|36|29|14.7|N|138|8|31.08|E|scale:30000}} | deities = {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto|ja|誉田別尊|12=draft}}
{{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|気長足姫尊|12=draft}}
{{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|大鷦鷯命|12=draft}} | founded = | main_hall= | other_name = Wakamiya Hachiman | main_festival = September 23 | events = }} '''Sarashina Shrine''' (さらしなじんじゃ) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Chikuma|ja|千曲市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|12=draft}}. It is a {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社|12=draft}} listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|12=draft}}, and under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=draft}}, it held the status of a ''gōsha'' (village shrine). == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto|ja|誉田別尊|12=draft}} * {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|気長足姫尊|12=draft}} * {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|大鷦鷯命|12=draft}} == History == According to shrine tradition, it was initially enshrined by {{ill|Prince Sakaibe no Kurohiko|ja|坂合黒彦皇子|12=draft}} on a ridge of the Sarashina mountains. However, following a landslide caused by an earthquake in the third year of the {{ill|Ninna era|ja|仁和|12=draft}} (887 CE), the shrine was relocated to the mountain's base. The shrine is referred to as "Wakamiya" in the {{ill|Heike Monogatari|ja|平家物語|12=draft}}, specifically in relation to an event in the first year of the {{ill|Yōwa era|ja|養和|12=draft}} when {{ill|Kiso Yoshinaka|ja|木曽義仲|12=draft}} reportedly "prostrated himself before Hachiman Shrine." It was known as Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine until the {{ill|Hōreki era|ja|宝暦|12=draft}} (1757 CE), when it assumed its current name. By 1759, records of the {{ill|Matsushiro Domain|ja|松代藩|12=draft}} noted the shrine’s landholdings at three ''koku''. Before the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離令|12=draft}} (separation of Shinto and Buddhism), the shrine was associated with a {{ill|Shingon sect temple|ja|真言宗|12=draft}} known as Wakamiya Hachiman Jingū-ji. File:Sarashina-jinja torii.jpg|Torii File:Sarashina-jinja 2.jpg|Main shrine building File:Sarashina-jinja cenotaph.jpg|{{ill|Cenotaph for loyal souls|ja|忠魂碑|12=draft}} * '''Treasure Tower (Hōkyōintō):''' The grounds contain an ancient stone pagoda inscribed with the year {{ill|Eiwa era|ja|永和|12=draft}} 2 (1378 CE). It is believed to mark the burial site of 45 local warriors who pledged their loyalty to Prince {{ill|Morinaga|ja|宗良親王|12=draft}} and prepared for death before heading to battle. It was designated a tangible cultural property of the city in 1988. * '''Earthworks:''' The northern and eastern sides of the shrine grounds retain traces of medieval fortifications, possibly linked to a {{ill|Southern Court (Japan)|ja|南朝|12=draft}} loyalist stronghold. * '''Slender Bronze Sword:''' Discovered during the Edo period in the nearby {{ill|Yazuka Ruins|ja|箭塚遺跡|12=draft}}, this sword became a sacred treasure of the shrine. It drew attention from historians for demonstrating that bronze cultures from {{ill|Northern Kyushu|ja|北九州|12=draft}} extended far beyond the {{ill|Kansai region|ja|近畿圏|12=draft}}. It was designated a tangible cultural property of the city in 1988. * '''Torii Gate:''' The current torii faces east towards the {{ill|Chikuma River|ja|千曲川|12=draft}}, which historically marked the rear of the shrine. The traditional approach was from the west, via the mountains and the ancient Hachioji-san Manji Pass. The shrine once served the worship needs of the three former villages of Wakamiya, Kurohiko, and Shibahara. Of these, Kurohiko Village, with a population of 1,000 non-agricultural households, was reportedly devastated and dispersed by frequent floods during the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|12=draft}}. The names of Kurohiko and Wakamiya are thought to derive from the deeds of the two princes linked to the shrine's origins. == References == == Bibliography == * ''Shrines and Temples in the Shinano Cultural Series,'' 1981, {{ill|Shinano Mainichi Shimbun|ja|信濃毎日新聞社|12=draft}}. * ''{{ill|Kadokawa Japan Place names Dictionary|ja|角川日本地名大辞典|12=draft}} Vol. 20: Nagano Prefecture,'' 1990, {{ill|Kadokawa Shoten|ja|角川書店|12=draft}}. {{Commonscat}} {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=Nagano Prefecture}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Sarashina Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gōsha shrines]] [[Category:Shikinai shrines in Shinano Province]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Chikuma, Nagano]] [[Category:History of Chikuma, Nagano]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[sn:Sarashina Shrine]] [[ja:佐良志奈神社]] [[en:Sarashina Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Sarashina Shrine}}

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sarashina Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Sarashina-jinja 1.jpg|300px]] | location = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|12=draft}}, {{ill|Chikuma|ja|千曲市|12=draft}}, Wakamiya 2 | coordinates = {{Coord|36|29|14.7|N|138|8|31.08|E|scale:30000}} | deities = {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto|ja|誉田別尊|12=draft}}
{{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|気長足姫尊|12=draft}}
{{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|大鷦鷯命|12=draft}} | founded = | main_hall= | other_name = Wakamiya Hachiman | main_festival = September 23 | events = }} '''Sarashina Shrine''' (さらしなじんじゃ) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Chikuma|ja|千曲市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|12=draft}}. It is a {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社|12=draft}} listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|12=draft}}, and under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=draft}}, it held the status of a ''gōsha'' (village shrine). == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto|ja|誉田別尊|12=draft}} * {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|気長足姫尊|12=draft}} * {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|大鷦鷯命|12=draft}} == History == According to shrine tradition, it was initially enshrined by {{ill|Prince Sakaibe no Kurohiko|ja|坂合黒彦皇子|12=draft}} on a ridge of the Sarashina mountains. However, following a landslide caused by an earthquake in the third year of the {{ill|Ninna era|ja|仁和|12=draft}} (887 CE), the shrine was relocated to the mountain's base. The shrine is referred to as "Wakamiya" in the {{ill|Heike Monogatari|ja|平家物語|12=draft}}, specifically in relation to an event in the first year of the {{ill|Yōwa era|ja|養和|12=draft}} when {{ill|Kiso Yoshinaka|ja|木曽義仲|12=draft}} reportedly "prostrated himself before Hachiman Shrine." It was known as Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine until the {{ill|Hōreki era|ja|宝暦|12=draft}} (1757 CE), when it assumed its current name. By 1759, records of the {{ill|Matsushiro Domain|ja|松代藩|12=draft}} noted the shrine’s landholdings at three ''koku''. Before the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離令|12=draft}} (separation of Shinto and Buddhism), the shrine was associated with a {{ill|Shingon sect temple|ja|真言宗|12=draft}} known as Wakamiya Hachiman Jingū-ji. File:Sarashina-jinja torii.jpg|Torii File:Sarashina-jinja 2.jpg|Main shrine building File:Sarashina-jinja cenotaph.jpg|{{ill|Cenotaph for loyal souls|ja|忠魂碑|12=draft}} * '''Treasure Tower (Hōkyōintō):''' The grounds contain an ancient stone pagoda inscribed with the year {{ill|Eiwa era|ja|永和|12=draft}} 2 (1378 CE). It is believed to mark the burial site of 45 local warriors who pledged their loyalty to Prince {{ill|Morinaga|ja|宗良親王|12=draft}} and prepared for death before heading to battle. It was designated a tangible cultural property of the city in 1988. * '''Earthworks:''' The northern and eastern sides of the shrine grounds retain traces of medieval fortifications, possibly linked to a {{ill|Southern Court (Japan)|ja|南朝|12=draft}} loyalist stronghold. * '''Slender Bronze Sword:''' Discovered during the Edo period in the nearby {{ill|Yazuka Ruins|ja|箭塚遺跡|12=draft}}, this sword became a sacred treasure of the shrine. It drew attention from historians for demonstrating that bronze cultures from {{ill|Northern Kyushu|ja|北九州|12=draft}} extended far beyond the {{ill|Kansai region|ja|近畿圏|12=draft}}. It was designated a tangible cultural property of the city in 1988. * '''Torii Gate:''' The current torii faces east towards the {{ill|Chikuma River|ja|千曲川|12=draft}}, which historically marked the rear of the shrine. The traditional approach was from the west, via the mountains and the ancient Hachioji-san Manji Pass. The shrine once served the worship needs of the three former villages of Wakamiya, Kurohiko, and Shibahara. Of these, Kurohiko Village, with a population of 1,000 non-agricultural households, was reportedly devastated and dispersed by frequent floods during the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|12=draft}}. The names of Kurohiko and Wakamiya are thought to derive from the deeds of the two princes linked to the shrine's origins. == References == == Bibliography == * ''Shrines and Temples in the Shinano Cultural Series,'' 1981, {{ill|Shinano Mainichi Shimbun|ja|信濃毎日新聞社|12=draft}}. * ''{{ill|Kadokawa Japan Place names Dictionary|ja|角川日本地名大辞典|12=draft}} Vol. 20: Nagano Prefecture,'' 1990, {{ill|Kadokawa Shoten|ja|角川書店|12=draft}}. {{Commonscat}} {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=Nagano Prefecture}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Sarashina Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gōsha shrines]] [[Category:Shikinai shrines in Shinano Province]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Chikuma, Nagano]] [[Category:History of Chikuma, Nagano]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Sarashina Shrine]] [[ja:佐良志奈神社]] [[en:Sarashina Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Sarashina Shrine}}

It was known as Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine until the {{ill|Hōreki era|ja|宝暦|12=draft|12=draft}} (1757 CE), when it assumed its current name. By 1759, records of the {{ill|Matsushiro Domain|ja|松代藩|12=draft|12=draft}} noted the shrine’s landholdings at three ''koku''. Before the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離令|12=draft|12=draft}} (separation of Shinto and Buddhism), the shrine was associated with a {{ill|Shingon sect temple|ja|真言宗|12=draft|12=draft}} known as Wakamiya Hachiman Jingū-ji. File:Sarashina-jinja torii.jpg|Torii File:Sarashina-jinja 2.jpg|Main shrine building File:Sarashina-jinja cenotaph.jpg|{{ill|Cenotaph for loyal souls|ja|忠魂碑|12=draft|12=draft}} * '''Treasure Tower (Hōkyōintō):''' The grounds contain an ancient stone pagoda inscribed with the year {{ill|Eiwa era|ja|永和|12=draft|12=draft}} 2 (1378 CE). It is believed to mark the burial site of 45 local warriors who pledged their loyalty to Prince {{ill|Morinaga|ja|宗良親王|12=draft|12=draft}} and prepared for death before heading to battle. It was designated a tangible cultural property of the city in 1988. * '''Earthworks:''' The northern and eastern sides of the shrine grounds retain traces of medieval fortifications, possibly linked to a {{ill|Southern Court (Japan)|ja|南朝|12=draft|12=draft}} loyalist stronghold. * '''Slender Bronze Sword:''' Discovered during the Edo period in the nearby {{ill|Yazuka Ruins|ja|箭塚遺跡|12=draft|12=draft}}, this sword became a sacred treasure of the shrine. It drew attention from historians for demonstrating that bronze cultures from {{ill|Northern Kyushu|ja|北九州|12=draft|12=draft}} extended far beyond the {{ill|Kansai region|ja|近畿圏|12=draft|12=draft}}. It was designated a tangible cultural property of the city in 1988. * '''Torii Gate:''' The current torii faces east towards the {{ill|Chikuma River|ja|千曲川|12=draft|12=draft}}, which historically marked the rear of the shrine. The traditional approach was from the west, via the mountains and the ancient Hachioji-san Manji Pass. The shrine once served the worship needs of the three former villages of Wakamiya, Kurohiko, and Shibahara. Of these, Kurohiko Village, with a population of 1,000 non-agricultural households, was reportedly devastated and dispersed by frequent floods during the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|12=draft|12=draft}}. The names of Kurohiko and Wakamiya are thought to derive from the deeds of the two princes linked to the shrine's origins. == References == == Bibliography == * ''Shrines and Temples in the Shinano Cultural Series,'' 1981, {{ill|Shinano Mainichi Shimbun|ja|信濃毎日新聞社|12=draft|12=draft}}. * ''{{ill|Kadokawa Japan Place names Dictionary|ja|角川日本地名大辞典|12=draft|12=draft}} Vol. 20: Nagano Prefecture,'' 1990, {{ill|Kadokawa Shoten|ja|角川書店|12=draft|12=draft}}. {{Commonscat}} {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=Nagano Prefecture}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Sarashina Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gōsha shrines]] [[Category:Shikinai shrines in Shinano Province]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Chikuma, Nagano]] [[Category:History of Chikuma, Nagano]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Sarashina Shrine]] [[ja:佐良志奈神社]] [[en:Sarashina Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Sarashina Shrine}}

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sarashina Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Sarashina-jinja 1.jpg|300px]] | location = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Chikuma|ja|千曲市|12=draft|12=draft}}, Wakamiya 2 | coordinates = {{Coord|36|29|14.7|N|138|8|31.08|E|scale:30000}} | deities = {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto|ja|誉田別尊|12=draft|12=draft}}
{{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|気長足姫尊|12=draft|12=draft}}
{{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|大鷦鷯命|12=draft|12=draft}} | founded = | main_hall= | other_name = Wakamiya Hachiman | main_festival = September 23 | events = }} '''Sarashina Shrine''' (さらしなじんじゃ) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Chikuma|ja|千曲市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|12=draft|12=draft}}. It is a {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft|12=draft}} listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|12=draft|12=draft}}, and under the {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|12=draft|12=draft}}, it held the status of a ''gōsha'' (village shrine). == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto|ja|誉田別尊|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|気長足姫尊|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|大鷦鷯命|12=draft|12=draft}} == History == According to shrine tradition, it was initially enshrined by {{ill|Prince Sakaibe no Kurohiko|ja|坂合黒彦皇子|12=draft|12=draft}} on a ridge of the Sarashina mountains. However, following a landslide caused by an earthquake in the third year of the {{ill|Ninna era|ja|仁和|12=draft|12=draft}} (887 CE), the shrine was relocated to the mountain's base. The shrine is referred to as "Wakamiya" in the {{ill|Heike Monogatari|ja|平家物語|12=draft|12=draft}}, specifically in relation to an event in the first year of the {{ill|Yōwa era|ja|養和|12=draft|12=draft}} when {{ill|Kiso Yoshinaka|ja|木曽義仲|12=draft|12=draft}} reportedly "prostrated himself before Hachiman Shrine." It was known as Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine until the {{ill|Hōreki era|ja|宝暦|12=draft|12=draft}} (1757 CE), when it assumed its current name. By 1759, records of the {{ill|Matsushiro Domain|ja|松代藩|12=draft|12=draft}} noted the shrine’s landholdings at three ''koku''. Before the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離令|12=draft|12=draft}} (separation of Shinto and Buddhism), the shrine was associated with a {{ill|Shingon sect temple|ja|真言宗|12=draft|12=draft}} known as Wakamiya Hachiman Jingū-ji. File:Sarashina-jinja torii.jpg|Torii File:Sarashina-jinja 2.jpg|Main shrine building File:Sarashina-jinja cenotaph.jpg|{{ill|Cenotaph for loyal souls|ja|忠魂碑|12=draft|12=draft}} * '''Treasure Tower (Hōkyōintō):''' The grounds contain an ancient stone pagoda inscribed with the year {{ill|Eiwa era|ja|永和|12=draft|12=draft}} 2 (1378 CE). It is believed to mark the burial site of 45 local warriors who pledged their loyalty to Prince {{ill|Morinaga|ja|宗良親王|12=draft|12=draft}} and prepared for death before heading to battle. It was designated a tangible cultural property of the city in 1988. * '''Earthworks:''' The northern and eastern sides of the shrine grounds retain traces of medieval fortifications, possibly linked to a {{ill|Southern Court (Japan)|ja|南朝|12=draft|12=draft}} loyalist stronghold. * '''Slender Bronze Sword:''' Discovered during the Edo period in the nearby {{ill|Yazuka Ruins|ja|箭塚遺跡|12=draft|12=draft}}, this sword became a sacred treasure of the shrine. It drew attention from historians for demonstrating that bronze cultures from {{ill|Northern Kyushu|ja|北九州|12=draft|12=draft}} extended far beyond the {{ill|Kansai region|ja|近畿圏|12=draft|12=draft}}. It was designated a tangible cultural property of the city in 1988. * '''Torii Gate:''' The current torii faces east towards the {{ill|Chikuma River|ja|千曲川|12=draft|12=draft}}, which historically marked the rear of the shrine. The traditional approach was from the west, via the mountains and the ancient Hachioji-san Manji Pass. The shrine once served the worship needs of the three former villages of Wakamiya, Kurohiko, and Shibahara. Of these, Kurohiko Village, with a population of 1,000 non-agricultural households, was reportedly devastated and dispersed by frequent floods during the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|12=draft|12=draft}}. The names of Kurohiko and Wakamiya are thought to derive from the deeds of the two princes linked to the shrine's origins. == References == == Bibliography == * ''Shrines and Temples in the Shinano Cultural Series,'' 1981, {{ill|Shinano Mainichi Shimbun|ja|信濃毎日新聞社|12=draft|12=draft}}. * ''{{ill|Kadokawa Japan Place names Dictionary|ja|角川日本地名大辞典|12=draft|12=draft}} Vol. 20: Nagano Prefecture,'' 1990, {{ill|Kadokawa Shoten|ja|角川書店|12=draft|12=draft}}. {{Commonscat}} {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=Nagano Prefecture}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Sarashina Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gōsha shrines]] [[Category:Shikinai shrines in Shinano Province]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Chikuma, Nagano]] [[Category:History of Chikuma, Nagano]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Sarashina Shrine]] [[ja:佐良志奈神社]] [[en:Sarashina Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Sarashina Shrine}}

{{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|大鷦鷯命|12=draft|12=draft}} | founded = | main_hall= | other_name = Wakamiya Hachiman | main_festival = September 23 | events = }} '''Sarashina Shrine''' (さらしなじんじゃ) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Chikuma|ja|千曲市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|12=draft|12=draft}}. It is a {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft|12=draft}} listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|12=draft|12=draft}}, and under the {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|12=draft|12=draft}}, it held the status of a ''gōsha'' (village shrine). == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto|ja|誉田別尊|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|気長足姫尊|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|大鷦鷯命|12=draft|12=draft}} == History == According to shrine tradition, it was initially enshrined by {{ill|Prince Sakaibe no Kurohiko|ja|坂合黒彦皇子|12=draft|12=draft}} on a ridge of the Sarashina mountains. However, following a landslide caused by an earthquake in the third year of the {{ill|Ninna era|ja|仁和|12=draft|12=draft}} (887 CE), the shrine was relocated to the mountain's base. The shrine is referred to as "Wakamiya" in the {{ill|Heike Monogatari|ja|平家物語|12=draft|12=draft}}, specifically in relation to an event in the first year of the {{ill|Yōwa era|ja|養和|12=draft|12=draft}} when {{ill|Kiso Yoshinaka|ja|木曽義仲|12=draft|12=draft}} reportedly "prostrated himself before Hachiman Shrine." It was known as Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine until the {{ill|Hōreki era|ja|宝暦|12=draft|12=draft}} (1757 CE), when it assumed its current name. By 1759, records of the {{ill|Matsushiro Domain|ja|松代藩|12=draft|12=draft}} noted the shrine’s landholdings at three ''koku''. Before the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離令|12=draft|12=draft}} (separation of Shinto and Buddhism), the shrine was associated with a {{ill|Shingon sect temple|ja|真言宗|12=draft|12=draft}} known as Wakamiya Hachiman Jingū-ji. File:Sarashina-jinja torii.jpg|Torii File:Sarashina-jinja 2.jpg|Main shrine building File:Sarashina-jinja cenotaph.jpg|{{ill|Cenotaph for loyal souls|ja|忠魂碑|12=draft|12=draft}} * '''Treasure Tower (Hōkyōintō):''' The grounds contain an ancient stone pagoda inscribed with the year {{ill|Eiwa era|ja|永和|12=draft|12=draft}} 2 (1378 CE). It is believed to mark the burial site of 45 local warriors who pledged their loyalty to Prince {{ill|Morinaga|ja|宗良親王|12=draft|12=draft}} and prepared for death before heading to battle. It was designated a tangible cultural property of the city in 1988. * '''Earthworks:''' The northern and eastern sides of the shrine grounds retain traces of medieval fortifications, possibly linked to a {{ill|Southern Court (Japan)|ja|南朝|12=draft|12=draft}} loyalist stronghold. * '''Slender Bronze Sword:''' Discovered during the Edo period in the nearby {{ill|Yazuka Ruins|ja|箭塚遺跡|12=draft|12=draft}}, this sword became a sacred treasure of the shrine. It drew attention from historians for demonstrating that bronze cultures from {{ill|Northern Kyushu|ja|北九州|12=draft|12=draft}} extended far beyond the {{ill|Kansai region|ja|近畿圏|12=draft|12=draft}}. It was designated a tangible cultural property of the city in 1988. * '''Torii Gate:''' The current torii faces east towards the {{ill|Chikuma River|ja|千曲川|12=draft|12=draft}}, which historically marked the rear of the shrine. The traditional approach was from the west, via the mountains and the ancient Hachioji-san Manji Pass. The shrine once served the worship needs of the three former villages of Wakamiya, Kurohiko, and Shibahara. Of these, Kurohiko Village, with a population of 1,000 non-agricultural households, was reportedly devastated and dispersed by frequent floods during the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|12=draft|12=draft}}. The names of Kurohiko and Wakamiya are thought to derive from the deeds of the two princes linked to the shrine's origins. == References == == Bibliography == * ''Shrines and Temples in the Shinano Cultural Series,'' 1981, {{ill|Shinano Mainichi Shimbun|ja|信濃毎日新聞社|12=draft|12=draft}}. * ''{{ill|Kadokawa Japan Place names Dictionary|ja|角川日本地名大辞典|12=draft|12=draft}} Vol. 20: Nagano Prefecture,'' 1990, {{ill|Kadokawa Shoten|ja|角川書店|12=draft|12=draft}}. {{Commonscat}} {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=Nagano Prefecture}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Sarashina Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gōsha shrines]] [[Category:Shikinai shrines in Shinano Province]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Chikuma, Nagano]] [[Category:History of Chikuma, Nagano]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Sarashina Shrine]] [[ja:佐良志奈神社]] [[en:Sarashina Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Sarashina Shrine}}

{{神社 |名称 = 佐良志奈神社 |画像 = [[File:Sarashina-jinja 1.jpg|300px]] |所在地 = [[長野県]][[千曲市]]若宮2番地 |位置= {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|29|14.7|N|138|8|31.08|E|scale:30000}} |祭神 = [[応神天皇|誉田別尊]]
[[神功皇后|気長足姫尊]]
[[仁徳天皇|大鷦鷯命]]　 |社格 = |創建 = |本殿 = |別名 = 若宮八幡 |札所等 = |例祭 = 9月23日 |神事 = }} '''佐良志奈神社'''（さらしなじんじゃ）は[[長野県]][[千曲市]]にある神社。『[[延喜式神名帳]]』に記載される[[式内社]]である。[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は郷社。 == 祭神 == *[[応神天皇|誉田別尊]] *[[神功皇后|気長足姫尊]] *[[仁徳天皇|大鷦鷯命]] == 歴史 == 社伝によれば、当初は[[坂合黒彦皇子]]が勧請し、更級山地の支嶺に鎮座していたが、[[仁和]]3年（[[887年]]）の震災により社地が崩落し、山麓に遷座したとされる。 『[[源平盛衰記]]』（[[養和]]元年に[[源義仲|木曽義仲]]が「八幡社を伏拝み」とあるのは当社である事を否定できない）には「若宮」とあり「信州の文化シリーズ 寺と神社」p196、中世まで若宮八幡宮と称した。 [[宝暦]]7年（[[1757年]]）に現在の社名となった。同9年（[[1759年]]）の[[松代藩]]書上では社領3石であった「角川日本地名大辞典」 p.534。 また神仏分離令以前は[[真言宗|別当寺]]として[[真言宗]]の若宮八幡[[神宮寺]]があった。 File:Sarashina-jinja torii.jpg|鳥居 File:Sarashina-jinja 2.jpg|社殿 File:Sarashina-jinja cenotaph.jpg|[[忠魂碑]] *[[宝篋印塔]] - 境内には[[永和 (日本)|永和]]2年（1378年）銘の古い石塔が残されている。[[後醍醐天皇]]の皇子[[宗良親王]]に決死の従軍を誓った在地の武士４５名が戦地に赴く前に自分達の墓とした逆襲塚とされる。昭和６３年に市の有形文化財に指定されている。 *土塁跡 - 中世の城館跡を思わせる土塁跡が境内の北側と東側にある。[[南朝 (日本)|南朝]]方武士団の拠点であった名残りかも知れない。 *細形銅剣 - [[江戸時代]]の文化年間に近くの箭塚遺跡から発掘され、この神社の社宝とされた。[[昭和]]63年（1988年）に市の[[有形文化財]]に指定された。北九州が中心とされていた銅器文化が[[近畿圏]]を遥かに超えた当地で出土していたことについて歴史学会から注目されていた。 *かつて存在していた若宮村・黒彦村・芝原村の3か村で氏子を構成していたとされる。この内の旧黒彦村については1,000軒もの非農耕者の大きな村落であったが[[戦国時代 (日本)|戦国時代]]に度々の大洪水で流亡分散したと伝えられる。この黒彦と若宮の地名については神社の由来に関係する2人の皇子の事績に因むものと考えられている。 *鳥居 - 現在は東側の[[千曲川]]に面して建てられているが、かつてそこは神社の裏側に当たっていた。古くからの[[戸倉宿]]と[[明治]]になって開湯した[[上山田温泉]]とを結んぶ渡し舟はあったが[[戸倉駅]]の開設によって[[大正橋 (千曲市)|大正橋]]が架橋されて表側とされるようになった。本来の[[参道]]は西方の山側から入っていて古来は八王子山万治峠越えをする道からの参詣であった。 *洞窟 - 現在は塞がれているが境内南側から抜けて上山田側の明治新道に通じる[[地下通路]]があった。[[第二次大戦]]中に洞窟が掘られ[[軍需工場]]を設けて一応の完成を見たが、その翌日に終戦となった[[戦争遺跡]]と言われる。以後洞窟内に[[稲荷社]]が祀られたり、飲酒提供の店が営まれた時もあったが戦後２０年程で閉ざされたと伝わる。 *カタクリ祭り - 境内南面の北向き斜面に[[カタクリ]]の[[群生地]]があり「みどりのサポート隊」が管理し観察会を続ける。３月末から４月の第１週辺りが花の見頃でカタクリ祭りが設定され親しまれている。 == 脚注 == == 参考文献 == *『信州の文化シリーズ 寺と神社』 1981年 [[信濃毎日新聞|信濃毎日新聞社]] *『[[角川日本地名大辞典]] 20 長野県』 1990年 [[角川書店]] {{Commonscat}} {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=長野県}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:さらしなしんしや}} [[Category:長野県の神社]] [[Category:長野県の旧郷社]] [[Category:信濃国の式内社]] [[Category:千曲市の建築物]] [[Category:千曲市の歴史]]

The shrine once served the worship needs of the three former villages of Wakamiya, Kurohiko, and Shibahara. Of these, Kurohiko Village, with a population of 1,000 non-agricultural households, was reportedly devastated and dispersed by frequent floods during the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The names of Kurohiko and Wakamiya are thought to derive from the deeds of the two princes linked to the shrine's origins. == References == == Bibliography == * ''Shrines and Temples in the Shinano Cultural Series,'' 1981, {{ill|Shinano Mainichi Shimbun|ja|信濃毎日新聞社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * ''{{ill|Kadokawa Japan Place names Dictionary|ja|角川日本地名大辞典|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Vol. 20: Nagano Prefecture,'' 1990, {{ill|Kadokawa Shoten|ja|角川書店|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. {{Commonscat}} {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub-Nagano Prefecture}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Sarashina Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gōsha shrines]] [[Category:Shikinai shrines in Shinano Province]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Chikuma, Nagano]] [[Category:History of Chikuma, Nagano]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Sarashina Shrine]] [[ja:佐良志奈神社]] [[en:Sarashina Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Sarashina Shrine}}

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sarashina Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Sarashina-jinja 1.jpg|300px]] | location = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Chikuma|ja|千曲市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Wakamiya 2 | coordinates = {{Coord|36|29|14.7|N|138|8|31.08|E|scale:30000}} | deities = {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto|ja|誉田別尊|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
{{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|気長足姫尊|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
{{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|大鷦鷯命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | founded = | main_hall= | other_name = Wakamiya Hachiman | main_festival = September 23 | events = }} '''Sarashina Shrine''' (さらしなじんじゃ) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Chikuma|ja|千曲市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It is a {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}} listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and under the {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, it held the status of a ''gōsha'' (village shrine). == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto|ja|誉田別尊|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|気長足姫尊|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|大鷦鷯命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == History == According to shrine tradition, it was initially enshrined by {{ill|Prince Sakaibe no Kurohiko|ja|坂合黒彦皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on a ridge of the Sarashina mountains. However, following a landslide caused by an earthquake in the third year of the {{ill|Ninna era|ja|仁和|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (887 CE), the shrine was relocated to the mountain's base. The shrine is referred to as "Wakamiya" in the {{ill|Heike Monogatari|ja|平家物語|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, specifically in relation to an event in the first year of the {{ill|Yōwa era|ja|養和|qq=draft|qq=draft}} when {{ill|Kiso Yoshinaka|ja|木曽義仲|qq=draft|qq=draft}} reportedly "prostrated himself before Hachiman Shrine." It was known as Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine until the {{ill|Hōreki era|ja|宝暦|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (1757 CE), when it assumed its current name. By 1759, records of the {{ill|Matsushiro Domain|ja|松代藩|qq=draft|qq=draft}} noted the shrine’s landholdings at three ''koku''. Before the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離令|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (separation of Shinto and Buddhism), the shrine was associated with a {{ill|Shingon sect temple|ja|真言宗|qq=draft|qq=draft}} known as Wakamiya Hachiman Jingū-ji. File:Sarashina-jinja torii.jpg|Torii File:Sarashina-jinja 2.jpg|Main shrine building File:Sarashina-jinja cenotaph.jpg|{{ill|Cenotaph for loyal souls|ja|忠魂碑|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * '''Treasure Tower (Hōkyōintō):''' The grounds contain an ancient stone pagoda inscribed with the year {{ill|Eiwa era|ja|永和|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 2 (1378 CE). It is believed to mark the burial site of 45 local warriors who pledged their loyalty to Prince {{ill|Morinaga|ja|宗良親王|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and prepared for death before heading to battle. It was designated a tangible cultural property of the city in 1988. * '''Earthworks:''' The northern and eastern sides of the shrine grounds retain traces of medieval fortifications, possibly linked to a {{ill|Southern Court (Japan)|ja|南朝|qq=draft|qq=draft}} loyalist stronghold. * '''Slender Bronze Sword:''' Discovered during the Edo period in the nearby {{ill|Yazuka Ruins|ja|箭塚遺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, this sword became a sacred treasure of the shrine. It drew attention from historians for demonstrating that bronze cultures from {{ill|Northern Kyushu|ja|北九州|qq=draft|qq=draft}} extended far beyond the {{ill|Kansai region|ja|近畿圏|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It was designated a tangible cultural property of the city in 1988. * '''Torii Gate:''' The current torii faces east towards the {{ill|Chikuma River|ja|千曲川|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, which historically marked the rear of the shrine. The traditional approach was from the west, via the mountains and the ancient Hachioji-san Manji Pass. The shrine once served the worship needs of the three former villages of Wakamiya, Kurohiko, and Shibahara. Of these, Kurohiko Village, with a population of 1,000 non-agricultural households, was reportedly devastated and dispersed by frequent floods during the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The names of Kurohiko and Wakamiya are thought to derive from the deeds of the two princes linked to the shrine's origins. == References == == Bibliography == * ''Shrines and Temples in the Shinano Cultural Series,'' 1981, {{ill|Shinano Mainichi Shimbun|ja|信濃毎日新聞社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * ''{{ill|Kadokawa Japan Place names Dictionary|ja|角川日本地名大辞典|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Vol. 20: Nagano Prefecture,'' 1990, {{ill|Kadokawa Shoten|ja|角川書店|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. {{Commonscat}} {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub-Nagano Prefecture}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Sarashina Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gōsha shrines]] [[Category:Shikinai shrines in Shinano Province]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Chikuma, Nagano]] [[Category:History of Chikuma, Nagano]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Sarashina Shrine]] [[ja:佐良志奈神社]] [[en:Sarashina Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Sarashina Shrine}}

revision.comment: 27 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:佐良志奈神社 up to rev 104633418
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 佐良志奈神社 |画像 = [[File:Sarashina-jinja 1.jpg|300px]] |所在地 = [[長野県]][[千曲市]]若宮2番地 |位置= {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|29|14.7|N|138|8|31.08|E|scale:30000}} |祭神 = [[応神天皇|誉田別尊]]
[[神功皇后|気長足姫尊]]
[[仁徳天皇|大鷦鷯命]]　 |社格 = |創建 = |本殿 = |別名 = 若宮八幡 |札所等 = |例祭 = 9月23日 |神事 = }} '''佐良志奈神社'''（さらしなじんじゃ）は[[長野県]][[千曲市]]にある神社。『[[延喜式神名帳]]』に記載される[[式内社]]である。[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は郷社。 == 祭神 == *[[応神天皇|誉田別尊]] *[[神功皇后|気長足姫尊]] *[[仁徳天皇|大鷦鷯命]] == 歴史 == 社伝によれば、当初は[[坂合黒彦皇子]]が勧請し、更級山地の支嶺に鎮座していたが、[[仁和]]3年（[[887年]]）の震災により社地が崩落し、山麓に遷座したとされる。 『[[源平盛衰記]]』（[[養和]]元年に[[源義仲|木曽義仲]]が「八幡社を伏拝み」とあるのは当社である事を否定できない）には「若宮」とあり「信州の文化シリーズ 寺と神社」p196、中世まで若宮八幡宮と称した。 [[宝暦]]7年（[[1757年]]）に現在の社名となった。同9年（[[1759年]]）の[[松代藩]]書上では社領3石であった「角川日本地名大辞典」 p.534。 また神仏分離令以前は[[真言宗|別当寺]]として[[真言宗]]の若宮八幡[[神宮寺]]があった。 File:Sarashina-jinja torii.jpg|鳥居 File:Sarashina-jinja 2.jpg|社殿 File:Sarashina-jinja cenotaph.jpg|[[忠魂碑]] *[[宝篋印塔]] - 境内には[[永和 (日本)|永和]]2年（1378年）銘の古い石塔が残されている。[[後醍醐天皇]]の皇子[[宗良親王]]に決死の従軍を誓った在地の武士４５名が戦地に赴く前に自分達の墓とした逆襲塚とされる。昭和６３年に市の有形文化財に指定されている。 *土塁跡 - 中世の城館跡を思わせる土塁跡が境内の北側と東側にある。[[南朝 (日本)|南朝]]方武士団の拠点であった名残りかも知れない。 *細形銅剣 - [[江戸時代]]の文化年間に近くの箭塚遺跡から発掘され、この神社の社宝とされた。[[昭和]]63年（1988年）に市の[[有形文化財]]に指定された。北九州が中心とされていた銅器文化が[[近畿圏]]を遥かに超えた当地で出土していたことについて歴史学会から注目されていた。 *かつて存在していた若宮村・黒彦村・芝原村の3か村で氏子を構成していたとされる。この内の旧黒彦村については1,000軒もの非農耕者の大きな村落であったが[[戦国時代 (日本)|戦国時代]]に度々の大洪水で流亡分散したと伝えられる。この黒彦と若宮の地名については神社の由来に関係する2人の皇子の事績に因むものと考えられている。 *鳥居 - 現在は東側の[[千曲川]]に面して建てられているが、かつてそこは神社の裏側に当たっていた。古くからの[[戸倉宿]]と[[明治]]になって開湯した[[上山田温泉]]とを結んぶ渡し舟はあったが[[戸倉駅]]の開設によって[[大正橋 (千曲市)|大正橋]]が架橋されて表側とされるようになった。本来の[[参道]]は西方の山側から入っていて古来は八王子山万治峠越えをする道からの参詣であった。 *洞窟 - 現在は塞がれているが境内南側から抜けて上山田側の明治新道に通じる[[地下通路]]があった。[[第二次大戦]]中に洞窟が掘られ[[軍需工場]]を設けて一応の完成を見たが、その翌日に終戦となった[[戦争遺跡]]と言われる。以後洞窟内に[[稲荷社]]が祀られたり、飲酒提供の店が営まれた時もあったが戦後２０年程で閉ざされたと伝わる。 *カタクリ祭り - 境内南面の北向き斜面に[[カタクリ]]の[[群生地]]があり「みどりのサポート隊」が管理し観察会を続ける。３月末から４月の第１週辺りが花の見頃でカタクリ祭りが設定され親しまれている。 == 脚注 == == 参考文献 == *『信州の文化シリーズ 寺と神社』 1981年 [[信濃毎日新聞|信濃毎日新聞社]] *『[[角川日本地名大辞典]] 20 長野県』 1990年 [[角川書店]] {{Commonscat}} {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=長野県}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:さらしなしんしや}} [[Category:長野県の神社]] [[Category:長野県の旧郷社]] [[Category:信濃国の式内社]] [[Category:千曲市の建築物]] [[Category:千曲市の歴史]]

[[神功皇后|気長足姫尊]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sarashina Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Sarashina-jinja 1.jpg|300px]] | location = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Chikuma|ja|千曲市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Wakamiya 2 | coordinates = {{Coord|36|29|14.7|N|138|8|31.08|E|scale:30000}} | deities = {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto|ja|誉田別尊|qq=draft|qq=draft}}

However, following a landslide caused by an earthquake in the third year of the {{ill|Ninna era|ja|仁和|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (887 CE), the shrine was relocated to the mountain's base. The shrine is referred to as "Wakamiya" in the {{ill|Heike Monogatari|ja|平家物語|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, specifically in relation to an event in the first year of the {{ill|Yōwa era|ja|養和|qq=draft|qq=draft}} when {{ill|Kiso Yoshinaka|ja|木曽義仲|qq=draft|qq=draft}} reportedly "prostrated himself before Hachiman Shrine." It was known as Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine until the {{ill|Hōreki era|ja|宝暦|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (1757 CE), when it assumed its current name. By 1759, records of the {{ill|Matsushiro Domain|ja|松代藩|qq=draft|qq=draft}} noted the shrine’s landholdings at three ''koku''. Before the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離令|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (separation of Shinto and Buddhism), the shrine was associated with a {{ill|Shingon sect temple|ja|真言宗|qq=draft|qq=draft}} known as Wakamiya Hachiman Jingū-ji. File:Sarashina-jinja torii.jpg|Torii File:Sarashina-jinja 2.jpg|Main shrine building File:Sarashina-jinja cenotaph.jpg|{{ill|Cenotaph for loyal souls|ja|忠魂碑|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * '''Treasure Tower (Hōkyōintō):''' The grounds contain an ancient stone pagoda inscribed with the year {{ill|Eiwa era|ja|永和|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 2 (1378 CE). It is believed to mark the burial site of 45 local warriors who pledged their loyalty to Prince {{ill|Morinaga|ja|宗良親王|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and prepared for death before heading to battle. It was designated a tangible cultural property of the city in 1988. * '''Earthworks:''' The northern and eastern sides of the shrine grounds retain traces of medieval fortifications, possibly linked to a {{ill|Southern Court (Japan)|ja|南朝|qq=draft|qq=draft}} loyalist stronghold. * '''Slender Bronze Sword:''' Discovered during the Edo period in the nearby {{ill|Yazuka Ruins|ja|箭塚遺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, this sword became a sacred treasure of the shrine. It drew attention from historians for demonstrating that bronze cultures from {{ill|Northern Kyushu|ja|北九州|qq=draft|qq=draft}} extended far beyond the {{ill|Kansai region|ja|近畿圏|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It was designated a tangible cultural property of the city in 1988. * '''Torii Gate:''' The current torii faces east towards the {{ill|Chikuma River|ja|千曲川|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, which historically marked the rear of the shrine. The traditional approach was from the west, via the mountains and the ancient Hachioji-san Manji Pass. The shrine once served the worship needs of the three former villages of Wakamiya, Kurohiko, and Shibahara. Of these, Kurohiko Village, with a population of 1,000 non-agricultural households, was reportedly devastated and dispersed by frequent floods during the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The names of Kurohiko and Wakamiya are thought to derive from the deeds of the two princes linked to the shrine's origins. == References == == Bibliography == * ''Shrines and Temples in the Shinano Cultural Series,'' 1981, {{ill|Shinano Mainichi Shimbun|ja|信濃毎日新聞社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * ''{{ill|Kadokawa Japan Place names Dictionary|ja|角川日本地名大辞典|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Vol. 20: Nagano Prefecture,'' 1990, {{ill|Kadokawa Shoten|ja|角川書店|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. {{Commonscat}} {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub-Nagano Prefecture}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Sarashina Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gōsha shrines]] [[Category:Shikinai shrines in Shinano Province]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Chikuma, Nagano]] [[Category:History of Chikuma, Nagano]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Sarashina Shrine]] [[ja:佐良志奈神社]] [[en:Sarashina Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Sarashina Shrine}} {{translated page|ja|佐良志奈神社|version=104633418|comment=Imported full ja history}}

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sarashina Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Sarashina-jinja 1.jpg|300px]] | location = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Chikuma|ja|千曲市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Wakamiya 2 | coordinates = {{Coord|36|29|14.7|N|138|8|31.08|E|scale:30000}} | deities = {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto|ja|誉田別尊|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
{{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|気長足姫尊|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
{{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|大鷦鷯命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | founded = | main_hall= | other_name = Wakamiya Hachiman | main_festival = September 23 | events = }} '''Sarashina Shrine''' (さらしなじんじゃ) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Chikuma|ja|千曲市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It is a {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}} listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and under the {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, it held the status of a ''gōsha'' (village shrine). == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto|ja|誉田別尊|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|気長足姫尊|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|大鷦鷯命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == History == According to shrine tradition, it was initially enshrined by {{ill|Prince Sakaibe no Kurohiko|ja|坂合黒彦皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on a ridge of the Sarashina mountains. However, following a landslide caused by an earthquake in the third year of the {{ill|Ninna era|ja|仁和|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (887 CE), the shrine was relocated to the mountain's base. The shrine is referred to as "Wakamiya" in the {{ill|Heike Monogatari|ja|平家物語|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, specifically in relation to an event in the first year of the {{ill|Yōwa era|ja|養和|qq=draft|qq=draft}} when {{ill|Kiso Yoshinaka|ja|木曽義仲|qq=draft|qq=draft}} reportedly "prostrated himself before Hachiman Shrine." It was known as Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine until the {{ill|Hōreki era|ja|宝暦|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (1757 CE), when it assumed its current name. By 1759, records of the {{ill|Matsushiro Domain|ja|松代藩|qq=draft|qq=draft}} noted the shrine’s landholdings at three ''koku''. Before the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離令|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (separation of Shinto and Buddhism), the shrine was associated with a {{ill|Shingon sect temple|ja|真言宗|qq=draft|qq=draft}} known as Wakamiya Hachiman Jingū-ji. File:Sarashina-jinja torii.jpg|Torii File:Sarashina-jinja 2.jpg|Main shrine building File:Sarashina-jinja cenotaph.jpg|{{ill|Cenotaph for loyal souls|ja|忠魂碑|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * '''Treasure Tower (Hōkyōintō):''' The grounds contain an ancient stone pagoda inscribed with the year {{ill|Eiwa era|ja|永和|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 2 (1378 CE). It is believed to mark the burial site of 45 local warriors who pledged their loyalty to Prince {{ill|Morinaga|ja|宗良親王|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and prepared for death before heading to battle. It was designated a tangible cultural property of the city in 1988. * '''Earthworks:''' The northern and eastern sides of the shrine grounds retain traces of medieval fortifications, possibly linked to a {{ill|Southern Court (Japan)|ja|南朝|qq=draft|qq=draft}} loyalist stronghold. * '''Slender Bronze Sword:''' Discovered during the Edo period in the nearby {{ill|Yazuka Ruins|ja|箭塚遺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, this sword became a sacred treasure of the shrine. It drew attention from historians for demonstrating that bronze cultures from {{ill|Northern Kyushu|ja|北九州|qq=draft|qq=draft}} extended far beyond the {{ill|Kansai region|ja|近畿圏|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It was designated a tangible cultural property of the city in 1988. * '''Torii Gate:''' The current torii faces east towards the {{ill|Chikuma River|ja|千曲川|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, which historically marked the rear of the shrine. The traditional approach was from the west, via the mountains and the ancient Hachioji-san Manji Pass. The shrine once served the worship needs of the three former villages of Wakamiya, Kurohiko, and Shibahara. Of these, Kurohiko Village, with a population of 1,000 non-agricultural households, was reportedly devastated and dispersed by frequent floods during the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The names of Kurohiko and Wakamiya are thought to derive from the deeds of the two princes linked to the shrine's origins. == References == == Bibliography == * ''Shrines and Temples in the Shinano Cultural Series,'' 1981, {{ill|Shinano Mainichi Shimbun|ja|信濃毎日新聞社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * ''{{ill|Kadokawa Japan Place names Dictionary|ja|角川日本地名大辞典|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Vol. 20: Nagano Prefecture,'' 1990, {{ill|Kadokawa Shoten|ja|角川書店|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. {{Commonscat}} {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub-Nagano Prefecture}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Sarashina Shrine}} {{translated page|ja|佐良志奈神社|version=104633418|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Sarashina Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gōsha shrines]] [[Category:Shikinai shrines in Shinano Province]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Chikuma, Nagano]] [[Category:History of Chikuma, Nagano]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Sarashina Shrine]] [[ja:佐良志奈神社]] [[en:Sarashina Shrine]]

{{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|大鷦鷯命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | founded = | main_hall= | other_name = Wakamiya Hachiman | main_festival = September 23 | events = }} '''Sarashina Shrine''' (さらしなじんじゃ) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Chikuma|ja|千曲市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It is a {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}} listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and under the {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, it held the status of a ''gōsha'' (village shrine). == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto|ja|誉田別尊|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|気長足姫尊|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|大鷦鷯命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == History == According to shrine tradition, it was initially enshrined by {{ill|Prince Sakaibe no Kurohiko|ja|坂合黒彦皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on a ridge of the Sarashina mountains. However, following a landslide caused by an earthquake in the third year of the {{ill|Ninna era|ja|仁和|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (887 CE), the shrine was relocated to the mountain's base. The shrine is referred to as "Wakamiya" in the {{ill|Heike Monogatari|ja|平家物語|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, specifically in relation to an event in the first year of the {{ill|Yōwa era|ja|養和|qq=draft|qq=draft}} when {{ill|Kiso Yoshinaka|ja|木曽義仲|qq=draft|qq=draft}} reportedly "prostrated himself before Hachiman Shrine." It was known as Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine until the {{ill|Hōreki era|ja|宝暦|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (1757 CE), when it assumed its current name. By 1759, records of the {{ill|Matsushiro Domain|ja|松代藩|qq=draft|qq=draft}} noted the shrine’s landholdings at three ''koku''. Before the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離令|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (separation of Shinto and Buddhism), the shrine was associated with a {{ill|Shingon sect temple|ja|真言宗|qq=draft|qq=draft}} known as Wakamiya Hachiman Jingū-ji. File:Sarashina-jinja torii.jpg|Torii File:Sarashina-jinja 2.jpg|Main shrine building File:Sarashina-jinja cenotaph.jpg|{{ill|Cenotaph for loyal souls|ja|忠魂碑|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * '''Treasure Tower (Hōkyōintō):''' The grounds contain an ancient stone pagoda inscribed with the year {{ill|Eiwa era|ja|永和|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 2 (1378 CE). It is believed to mark the burial site of 45 local warriors who pledged their loyalty to Prince {{ill|Morinaga|ja|宗良親王|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and prepared for death before heading to battle. It was designated a tangible cultural property of the city in 1988. * '''Earthworks:''' The northern and eastern sides of the shrine grounds retain traces of medieval fortifications, possibly linked to a {{ill|Southern Court (Japan)|ja|南朝|qq=draft|qq=draft}} loyalist stronghold. * '''Slender Bronze Sword:''' Discovered during the Edo period in the nearby {{ill|Yazuka Ruins|ja|箭塚遺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, this sword became a sacred treasure of the shrine. It drew attention from historians for demonstrating that bronze cultures from {{ill|Northern Kyushu|ja|北九州|qq=draft|qq=draft}} extended far beyond the {{ill|Kansai region|ja|近畿圏|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It was designated a tangible cultural property of the city in 1988. * '''Torii Gate:''' The current torii faces east towards the {{ill|Chikuma River|ja|千曲川|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, which historically marked the rear of the shrine. The traditional approach was from the west, via the mountains and the ancient Hachioji-san Manji Pass. The shrine once served the worship needs of the three former villages of Wakamiya, Kurohiko, and Shibahara. Of these, Kurohiko Village, with a population of 1,000 non-agricultural households, was reportedly devastated and dispersed by frequent floods during the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The names of Kurohiko and Wakamiya are thought to derive from the deeds of the two princes linked to the shrine's origins. == References == == Bibliography == * ''Shrines and Temples in the Shinano Cultural Series,'' 1981, {{ill|Shinano Mainichi Shimbun|ja|信濃毎日新聞社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * ''{{ill|Kadokawa Japan Place names Dictionary|ja|角川日本地名大辞典|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Vol. 20: Nagano Prefecture,'' 1990, {{ill|Kadokawa Shoten|ja|角川書店|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. {{Commonscat}} {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub-Nagano Prefecture}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Sarashina Shrine}} {{translated page|ja|佐良志奈神社|version=104633418|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Sarashina Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gōsha shrines]] [[Category:Shikinai shrines in Shinano Province]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Chikuma, Nagano]] [[Category:History of Chikuma, Nagano]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Sarashina Shrine]] [[ja:佐良志奈神社]] [[en:Sarashina Shrine]]

It is believed to mark the burial site of 45 local warriors who pledged their loyalty to Prince {{ill|Morinaga|ja|宗良親王|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and prepared for death before heading to battle. It was designated a tangible cultural property of the city in 1988. * '''Earthworks:''' The northern and eastern sides of the shrine grounds retain traces of medieval fortifications, possibly linked to a {{ill|Southern Court (Japan)|ja|南朝|qq=draft|qq=draft}} loyalist stronghold. * '''Slender Bronze Sword:''' Discovered during the Edo period in the nearby {{ill|Yazuka Ruins|ja|箭塚遺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, this sword became a sacred treasure of the shrine. It drew attention from historians for demonstrating that bronze cultures from {{ill|Northern Kyushu|ja|北九州|qq=draft|qq=draft}} extended far beyond the {{ill|Kansai region|ja|近畿圏|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It was designated a tangible cultural property of the city in 1988. * '''Torii Gate:''' The current torii faces east towards the {{ill|Chikuma River|ja|千曲川|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, which historically marked the rear of the shrine. The traditional approach was from the west, via the mountains and the ancient Hachioji-san Manji Pass. The shrine once served the worship needs of the three former villages of Wakamiya, Kurohiko, and Shibahara. Of these, Kurohiko Village, with a population of 1,000 non-agricultural households, was reportedly devastated and dispersed by frequent floods during the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The names of Kurohiko and Wakamiya are thought to derive from the deeds of the two princes linked to the shrine's origins. == References == == Bibliography == * ''Shrines and Temples in the Shinano Cultural Series,'' 1981, {{ill|Shinano Mainichi Shimbun|ja|信濃毎日新聞社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * ''{{ill|Kadokawa Japan Place names Dictionary|ja|角川日本地名大辞典|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Vol. 20: Nagano Prefecture,'' 1990, {{ill|Kadokawa Shoten|ja|角川書店|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. {{Commonscat}} {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub-Nagano Prefecture}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|佐良志奈神社|version=104633418|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Sarashina Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gōsha shrines]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shinano Province]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Chikuma, Nagano]] [[Category:History of Chikuma, Nagano]] }} [[sn:Sarashina Shrine]] [[ja:佐良志奈神社]] [[en:Sarashina Shrine]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sarashina Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Sarashina-jinja 1.jpg|300px]] | location = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Chikuma|ja|千曲市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Wakamiya 2 | coordinates = {{Coord|36|29|14.7|N|138|8|31.08|E|scale:30000}} | deities = {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto|ja|誉田別尊|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
{{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|気長足姫尊|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
{{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|大鷦鷯命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | founded = | main_hall= | other_name = Wakamiya Hachiman | main_festival = September 23 | events = }} '''Sarashina Shrine''' (さらしなじんじゃ) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Chikuma|ja|千曲市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It is a {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}} listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and under the {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, it held the status of a ''gōsha'' (village shrine). == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto|ja|誉田別尊|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|気長足姫尊|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|大鷦鷯命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == History == According to shrine tradition, it was initially enshrined by {{ill|Prince Sakaibe no Kurohiko|ja|坂合黒彦皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on a ridge of the Sarashina mountains. However, following a landslide caused by an earthquake in the third year of the {{ill|Ninna era|ja|仁和|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (887 CE), the shrine was relocated to the mountain's base. The shrine is referred to as "Wakamiya" in the {{ill|Heike Monogatari|ja|平家物語|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, specifically in relation to an event in the first year of the {{ill|Yōwa era|ja|養和|qq=draft|qq=draft}} when {{ill|Kiso Yoshinaka|ja|木曽義仲|qq=draft|qq=draft}} reportedly "prostrated himself before Hachiman Shrine." It was known as Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine until the {{ill|Hōreki era|ja|宝暦|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (1757 CE), when it assumed its current name. By 1759, records of the {{ill|Matsushiro Domain|ja|松代藩|qq=draft|qq=draft}} noted the shrine’s landholdings at three ''koku''. Before the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離令|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (separation of Shinto and Buddhism), the shrine was associated with a {{ill|Shingon sect temple|ja|真言宗|qq=draft|qq=draft}} known as Wakamiya Hachiman Jingū-ji. File:Sarashina-jinja torii.jpg|Torii File:Sarashina-jinja 2.jpg|Main shrine building File:Sarashina-jinja cenotaph.jpg|{{ill|Cenotaph for loyal souls|ja|忠魂碑|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * '''Treasure Tower (Hōkyōintō):''' The grounds contain an ancient stone pagoda inscribed with the year {{ill|Eiwa era|ja|永和|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 2 (1378 CE). It is believed to mark the burial site of 45 local warriors who pledged their loyalty to Prince {{ill|Morinaga|ja|宗良親王|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and prepared for death before heading to battle. It was designated a tangible cultural property of the city in 1988. * '''Earthworks:''' The northern and eastern sides of the shrine grounds retain traces of medieval fortifications, possibly linked to a {{ill|Southern Court (Japan)|ja|南朝|qq=draft|qq=draft}} loyalist stronghold. * '''Slender Bronze Sword:''' Discovered during the Edo period in the nearby {{ill|Yazuka Ruins|ja|箭塚遺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, this sword became a sacred treasure of the shrine. It drew attention from historians for demonstrating that bronze cultures from {{ill|Northern Kyushu|ja|北九州|qq=draft|qq=draft}} extended far beyond the {{ill|Kansai region|ja|近畿圏|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It was designated a tangible cultural property of the city in 1988. * '''Torii Gate:''' The current torii faces east towards the {{ill|Chikuma River|ja|千曲川|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, which historically marked the rear of the shrine. The traditional approach was from the west, via the mountains and the ancient Hachioji-san Manji Pass. The shrine once served the worship needs of the three former villages of Wakamiya, Kurohiko, and Shibahara. Of these, Kurohiko Village, with a population of 1,000 non-agricultural households, was reportedly devastated and dispersed by frequent floods during the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The names of Kurohiko and Wakamiya are thought to derive from the deeds of the two princes linked to the shrine's origins. == References == == Bibliography == * ''Shrines and Temples in the Shinano Cultural Series,'' 1981, {{ill|Shinano Mainichi Shimbun|ja|信濃毎日新聞社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * ''{{ill|Kadokawa Japan Place names Dictionary|ja|角川日本地名大辞典|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Vol. 20: Nagano Prefecture,'' 1990, {{ill|Kadokawa Shoten|ja|角川書店|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. {{Commonscat}} {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub-Nagano Prefecture}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|佐良志奈神社|version=104633418|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Sarashina Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gōsha shrines]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shinano Province]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Chikuma, Nagano]] [[Category:History of Chikuma, Nagano]] }} [[sn:Sarashina Shrine]] [[ja:佐良志奈神社]] [[en:Sarashina Shrine]]
