It was known as Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine until the [[:en:Hōreki|Hōreki era]] (1757 CE), when it assumed its current name. By 1759, records of the [[:en:Matsushiro Domain|Matsushiro Domain]] noted the shrine’s landholdings at three ''koku''. Before the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu bunri|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神仏分離}} (separation of Shinto and Buddhism), the shrine was associated with a [[:en:Shingon Buddhism|Shingon sect temple]] known as Wakamiya Hachiman Jingū-ji. File:Sarashina-jinja torii.jpg|Torii File:Sarashina-jinja 2.jpg|Main shrine building File:Sarashina-jinja cenotaph.jpg|{{ill|Cenotaph for loyal souls|ja|忠魂碑|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cenotaph for loyal souls|qq=}} * '''Treasure Tower (Hōkyōintō):''' The grounds contain an ancient stone pagoda inscribed with the year {{ill|Eiwa era|ja|永和|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eiwa era|1=Eiwa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11131293}} 2 (1378 CE). It is believed to mark the burial site of 45 local warriors who pledged their loyalty to Prince [[:en:Prince Munenaga|Morinaga]] and prepared for death before heading to battle. It was designated a tangible cultural property of the city in 1988. * '''Earthworks:''' The northern and eastern sides of the shrine grounds retain traces of medieval fortifications, possibly linked to a {{ill|Southern Court (Japan)|ja|南朝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Southern Court (Japan)|1=Southern Dynasties|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1206988}} loyalist stronghold. * '''Slender Bronze Sword:''' Discovered during the Edo period in the nearby {{ill|Yazuka Ruins|ja|箭塚遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yazuka Ruins|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, this sword became a sacred treasure of the shrine. It drew attention from historians for demonstrating that bronze cultures from [[:en:Northern Kyushu|Northern Kyushu]] extended far beyond the {{ill|Kansai region|ja|近畿圏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kansai region|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 京阪神|1=Keihanshin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1149484}}. It was designated a tangible cultural property of the city in 1988. * '''Torii Gate:''' The current torii faces east towards the {{ill|Chikuma River|ja|千曲川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikuma River|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 信濃川|1=Shinano River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q734455}}, which historically marked the rear of the shrine. The traditional approach was from the west, via the mountains and the ancient Hachioji-san Manji Pass. The shrine once served the worship needs of the three former villages of Wakamiya, Kurohiko, and Shibahara. Of these, Kurohiko Village, with a population of 1,000 non-agricultural households, was reportedly devastated and dispersed by frequent floods during the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period|1=Sengoku Period (Japan)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q117415390}}. The names of Kurohiko and Wakamiya are thought to derive from the deeds of the two princes linked to the shrine's origins. == References == == Bibliography == * ''Shrines and Temples in the Shinano Cultural Series,'' 1981, {{ill|Shinano Mainichi Shimbun|ja|信濃毎日新聞社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Mainichi Shimbun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 信濃毎日新聞|1=Shinano Mainichi Shimbun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11386181}}. * ''{{ill|Kadokawa Japan Place names Dictionary|ja|角川日本地名大辞典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kadokawa Japan Place names Dictionary|1=Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4093011}} Vol. 20: Nagano Prefecture,'' 1990, [[:en:Kadokawa Shoten|Kadokawa Shoten]]. {{Commonscat}} {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub-Nagano Prefecture}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|佐良志奈神社|version=104633418|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shinano Province]] }} [[ja:佐良志奈神社]] [[Category:Buildings in Chikuma]] [[Category:History of Chikuma]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Nagano Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Sarashina-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q105849251}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] {{wikidata link|Q1}} {{wikidata link|Q1}} {{wikidata link|Q1}} {{wikidata link|Q1}} {{wikidata link|Q1}}

[[sn:commons:Category:Sarashina-jinja]][[ja:佐良志奈神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q105849251]] Wikidata name: Sarashina-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:06, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sarashina Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Sarashina-jinja 1.jpg | location = [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]], [[:en:Chikuma, Nagano|Chikuma]], Wakamiya 2 | coordinates = {{Coord|36.487255|N|138.1418964|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deities = {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto|ja|誉田別尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 応神天皇|1=Ōjin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q317997}}
{{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|気長足姫尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Jingū|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神功皇后|1=Empress Jingū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q232803}}
{{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|大鷦鷯命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Nintoku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 仁徳天皇|1=Nintoku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q313119}} | founded = | main_hall= | other_name = Wakamiya Hachiman | main_festival = September 23 | events = | prefecture= {{ill|Chikuma|WD=Q179757}} }} '''Sarashina Shrine''' (さらしなじんじゃ) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Chikuma, Nagano|Chikuma]], [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, and under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]], it held the status of a ''gōsha'' (village shrine). == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto|ja|誉田別尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 応神天皇|1=Ōjin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q317997}} * {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|気長足姫尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Jingū|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神功皇后|1=Empress Jingū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q232803}} * {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|大鷦鷯命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Nintoku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 仁徳天皇|1=Nintoku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q313119}} == History == According to shrine tradition, it was initially enshrined by {{ill|Prince Sakaibe no Kurohiko|ja|坂合黒彦皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Sakaibe no Kurohiko|1=Prince Sakai no Kurohiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11429115}} on a ridge of the Sarashina mountains. However, following a landslide caused by an earthquake in the third year of the [[:en:Ninna|Ninna era]] (887 CE), the shrine was relocated to the mountain's base. The shrine is referred to as "Wakamiya" in the [[:en:The Tale of the Heike|Heike Monogatari]], specifically in relation to an event in the first year of the [[:en:Yōwa|Yōwa era]] when {{ill|Kiso Yoshinaka|ja|木曽義仲|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiso Yoshinaka|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 源義仲|1=Minamoto no Yoshinaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1139202}} reportedly "prostrated himself before Hachiman Shrine." It was known as Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine until the [[:en:Hōreki|Hōreki era]] (1757 CE), when it assumed its current name. By 1759, records of the [[:en:Matsushiro Domain|Matsushiro Domain]] noted the shrine’s landholdings at three ''koku''. Before the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu bunri|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神仏分離}} (separation of Shinto and Buddhism), the shrine was associated with a [[:en:Shingon Buddhism|Shingon sect temple]] known as Wakamiya Hachiman Jingū-ji. File:Sarashina-jinja torii.jpg|Torii File:Sarashina-jinja 2.jpg|Main shrine building File:Sarashina-jinja cenotaph.jpg|{{ill|Cenotaph for loyal souls|ja|忠魂碑|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cenotaph for loyal souls|qq=}} * '''Treasure Tower (Hōkyōintō):''' The grounds contain an ancient stone pagoda inscribed with the year {{ill|Eiwa era|ja|永和|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eiwa era|1=Eiwa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11131293}} 2 (1378 CE). It is believed to mark the burial site of 45 local warriors who pledged their loyalty to Prince [[:en:Prince Munenaga|Morinaga]] and prepared for death before heading to battle. It was designated a tangible cultural property of the city in 1988. * '''Earthworks:''' The northern and eastern sides of the shrine grounds retain traces of medieval fortifications, possibly linked to a {{ill|Southern Court (Japan)|ja|南朝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Southern Court (Japan)|1=Southern Dynasties|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1206988}} loyalist stronghold. * '''Slender Bronze Sword:''' Discovered during the Edo period in the nearby {{ill|Yazuka Ruins|ja|箭塚遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yazuka Ruins|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, this sword became a sacred treasure of the shrine. It drew attention from historians for demonstrating that bronze cultures from [[:en:Northern Kyushu|Northern Kyushu]] extended far beyond the {{ill|Kansai region|ja|近畿圏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kansai region|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 京阪神|1=Keihanshin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1149484}}. It was designated a tangible cultural property of the city in 1988. * '''Torii Gate:''' The current torii faces east towards the {{ill|Chikuma River|ja|千曲川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikuma River|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 信濃川|1=Shinano River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q734455}}, which historically marked the rear of the shrine. The traditional approach was from the west, via the mountains and the ancient Hachioji-san Manji Pass. The shrine once served the worship needs of the three former villages of Wakamiya, Kurohiko, and Shibahara. Of these, Kurohiko Village, with a population of 1,000 non-agricultural households, was reportedly devastated and dispersed by frequent floods during the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period|1=Sengoku Period (Japan)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q117415390}}. The names of Kurohiko and Wakamiya are thought to derive from the deeds of the two princes linked to the shrine's origins. == References == == Bibliography == * ''Shrines and Temples in the Shinano Cultural Series,'' 1981, {{ill|Shinano Mainichi Shimbun|ja|信濃毎日新聞社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Mainichi Shimbun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 信濃毎日新聞|1=Shinano Mainichi Shimbun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11386181}}. * ''{{ill|Kadokawa Japan Place names Dictionary|ja|角川日本地名大辞典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kadokawa Japan Place names Dictionary|1=Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4093011}} Vol. 20: Nagano Prefecture,'' 1990, [[:en:Kadokawa Shoten|Kadokawa Shoten]]. {{Commonscat}} {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub-Nagano Prefecture}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|佐良志奈神社|version=104633418|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shinano Province]] }} [[ja:佐良志奈神社]] [[Category:Buildings in Chikuma]] [[Category:History of Chikuma]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Nagano Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Sarashina-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q105849251}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

The names of Kurohiko and Wakamiya are thought to derive from the deeds of the two princes linked to the shrine's origins. == References == == Bibliography == * ''Shrines and Temples in the Shinano Cultural Series,'' 1981, {{ill|Shinano Mainichi Shimbun|ja|信濃毎日新聞社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Mainichi Shimbun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 信濃毎日新聞|1=Shinano Mainichi Shimbun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11386181}}. * ''{{ill|Kadokawa Japan Place names Dictionary|ja|角川日本地名大辞典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kadokawa Japan Place names Dictionary|1=Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4093011}} Vol. 20: Nagano Prefecture,'' 1990, [[:en:Kadokawa Shoten|Kadokawa Shoten]]. {{Commonscat}} {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub-Nagano Prefecture}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|佐良志奈神社|version=104633418|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shinano Province]] }} [[ja:佐良志奈神社]] [[Category:Buildings in Chikuma]] [[Category:History of Chikuma]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Nagano Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Sarashina-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q105849251}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

[[commons:Category:Sarashina-jinja]][[ja:佐良志奈神社]] [[sn:commons:Category:Sarashina-jinja]][[ja:佐良志奈神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q105849251]] Wikidata name: Sarashina-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:06, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sarashina Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Sarashina-jinja 1.jpg | location = [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]], [[:en:Chikuma, Nagano|Chikuma]], Wakamiya 2 | coordinates = {{Coord|36.487255|N|138.1418964|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deities = {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto|ja|誉田別尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 応神天皇|1=Ōjin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q317997}}
{{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|気長足姫尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Jingū|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神功皇后|1=Empress Jingū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q232803}}
{{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|大鷦鷯命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Nintoku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 仁徳天皇|1=Nintoku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q313119}} | founded = | main_hall= | other_name = Wakamiya Hachiman | main_festival = September 23 | events = | prefecture= {{ill|Chikuma|WD=Q179757}} }} '''Sarashina Shrine''' (さらしなじんじゃ) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Chikuma, Nagano|Chikuma]], [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, and under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]], it held the status of a ''gōsha'' (village shrine). == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto|ja|誉田別尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 応神天皇|1=Ōjin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q317997}} * {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|気長足姫尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Jingū|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神功皇后|1=Empress Jingū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q232803}} * {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|大鷦鷯命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Nintoku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 仁徳天皇|1=Nintoku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q313119}} == History == According to shrine tradition, it was initially enshrined by {{ill|Prince Sakaibe no Kurohiko|ja|坂合黒彦皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Sakaibe no Kurohiko|1=Prince Sakai no Kurohiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11429115}} on a ridge of the Sarashina mountains. However, following a landslide caused by an earthquake in the third year of the [[:en:Ninna|Ninna era]] (887 CE), the shrine was relocated to the mountain's base. The shrine is referred to as "Wakamiya" in the [[:en:The Tale of the Heike|Heike Monogatari]], specifically in relation to an event in the first year of the [[:en:Yōwa|Yōwa era]] when {{ill|Kiso Yoshinaka|ja|木曽義仲|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiso Yoshinaka|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 源義仲|1=Minamoto no Yoshinaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1139202}} reportedly "prostrated himself before Hachiman Shrine." It was known as Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine until the [[:en:Hōreki|Hōreki era]] (1757 CE), when it assumed its current name. By 1759, records of the [[:en:Matsushiro Domain|Matsushiro Domain]] noted the shrine’s landholdings at three ''koku''. Before the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu bunri|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神仏分離}} (separation of Shinto and Buddhism), the shrine was associated with a [[:en:Shingon Buddhism|Shingon sect temple]] known as Wakamiya Hachiman Jingū-ji. File:Sarashina-jinja torii.jpg|Torii File:Sarashina-jinja 2.jpg|Main shrine building File:Sarashina-jinja cenotaph.jpg|{{ill|Cenotaph for loyal souls|ja|忠魂碑|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cenotaph for loyal souls|qq=}} * '''Treasure Tower (Hōkyōintō):''' The grounds contain an ancient stone pagoda inscribed with the year {{ill|Eiwa era|ja|永和|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eiwa era|1=Eiwa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11131293}} 2 (1378 CE). It is believed to mark the burial site of 45 local warriors who pledged their loyalty to Prince [[:en:Prince Munenaga|Morinaga]] and prepared for death before heading to battle. It was designated a tangible cultural property of the city in 1988. * '''Earthworks:''' The northern and eastern sides of the shrine grounds retain traces of medieval fortifications, possibly linked to a {{ill|Southern Court (Japan)|ja|南朝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Southern Court (Japan)|1=Southern Dynasties|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1206988}} loyalist stronghold. * '''Slender Bronze Sword:''' Discovered during the Edo period in the nearby {{ill|Yazuka Ruins|ja|箭塚遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yazuka Ruins|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, this sword became a sacred treasure of the shrine. It drew attention from historians for demonstrating that bronze cultures from [[:en:Northern Kyushu|Northern Kyushu]] extended far beyond the {{ill|Kansai region|ja|近畿圏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kansai region|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 京阪神|1=Keihanshin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1149484}}. It was designated a tangible cultural property of the city in 1988. * '''Torii Gate:''' The current torii faces east towards the {{ill|Chikuma River|ja|千曲川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikuma River|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 信濃川|1=Shinano River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q734455}}, which historically marked the rear of the shrine. The traditional approach was from the west, via the mountains and the ancient Hachioji-san Manji Pass. The shrine once served the worship needs of the three former villages of Wakamiya, Kurohiko, and Shibahara. Of these, Kurohiko Village, with a population of 1,000 non-agricultural households, was reportedly devastated and dispersed by frequent floods during the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period|1=Sengoku Period (Japan)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q117415390}}. The names of Kurohiko and Wakamiya are thought to derive from the deeds of the two princes linked to the shrine's origins. == References == == Bibliography == * ''Shrines and Temples in the Shinano Cultural Series,'' 1981, {{ill|Shinano Mainichi Shimbun|ja|信濃毎日新聞社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Mainichi Shimbun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 信濃毎日新聞|1=Shinano Mainichi Shimbun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11386181}}. * ''{{ill|Kadokawa Japan Place names Dictionary|ja|角川日本地名大辞典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kadokawa Japan Place names Dictionary|1=Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4093011}} Vol. 20: Nagano Prefecture,'' 1990, [[:en:Kadokawa Shoten|Kadokawa Shoten]]. {{Commonscat}} {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub-Nagano Prefecture}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|佐良志奈神社|version=104633418|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shinano Province]] }} [[ja:佐良志奈神社]] [[Category:Buildings in Chikuma]] [[Category:History of Chikuma]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Nagano Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Sarashina-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q105849251}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

{{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|気長足姫尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Jingū|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神功皇后|1=Empress Jingū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q232803}}
{{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|大鷦鷯命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Nintoku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 仁徳天皇|1=Nintoku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q313119}} | founded = | main_hall= | other_name = Wakamiya Hachiman | main_festival = September 23 | events = | prefecture= {{ill|Chikuma|WD=Q179757}} }} '''Sarashina Shrine''' (さらしなじんじゃ) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Chikuma, Nagano|Chikuma]], [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, and under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]], it held the status of a ''gōsha'' (village shrine). == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto|ja|誉田別尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 応神天皇|1=Ōjin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q317997}} * {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|気長足姫尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Jingū|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神功皇后|1=Empress Jingū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q232803}} * {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|大鷦鷯命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Nintoku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 仁徳天皇|1=Nintoku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q313119}} == History == According to shrine tradition, it was initially enshrined by {{ill|Prince Sakaibe no Kurohiko|ja|坂合黒彦皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Sakaibe no Kurohiko|1=Prince Sakai no Kurohiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11429115}} on a ridge of the Sarashina mountains. However, following a landslide caused by an earthquake in the third year of the [[:en:Ninna|Ninna era]] (887 CE), the shrine was relocated to the mountain's base. The shrine is referred to as "Wakamiya" in the [[:en:The Tale of the Heike|Heike Monogatari]], specifically in relation to an event in the first year of the [[:en:Yōwa|Yōwa era]] when {{ill|Kiso Yoshinaka|ja|木曽義仲|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiso Yoshinaka|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 源義仲|1=Minamoto no Yoshinaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1139202}} reportedly "prostrated himself before Hachiman Shrine." It was known as Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine until the [[:en:Hōreki|Hōreki era]] (1757 CE), when it assumed its current name. By 1759, records of the [[:en:Matsushiro Domain|Matsushiro Domain]] noted the shrine’s landholdings at three ''koku''. Before the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu bunri|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神仏分離}} (separation of Shinto and Buddhism), the shrine was associated with a [[:en:Shingon Buddhism|Shingon sect temple]] known as Wakamiya Hachiman Jingū-ji. File:Sarashina-jinja torii.jpg|Torii File:Sarashina-jinja 2.jpg|Main shrine building File:Sarashina-jinja cenotaph.jpg|{{ill|Cenotaph for loyal souls|ja|忠魂碑|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cenotaph for loyal souls|qq=}} * '''Treasure Tower (Hōkyōintō):''' The grounds contain an ancient stone pagoda inscribed with the year {{ill|Eiwa era|ja|永和|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eiwa era|1=Eiwa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11131293}} 2 (1378 CE). It is believed to mark the burial site of 45 local warriors who pledged their loyalty to Prince [[:en:Prince Munenaga|Morinaga]] and prepared for death before heading to battle. It was designated a tangible cultural property of the city in 1988. * '''Earthworks:''' The northern and eastern sides of the shrine grounds retain traces of medieval fortifications, possibly linked to a {{ill|Southern Court (Japan)|ja|南朝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Southern Court (Japan)|1=Southern Dynasties|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1206988}} loyalist stronghold. * '''Slender Bronze Sword:''' Discovered during the Edo period in the nearby {{ill|Yazuka Ruins|ja|箭塚遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yazuka Ruins|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, this sword became a sacred treasure of the shrine. It was designated a tangible cultural property of the city in 1988. * '''Torii Gate:''' The current torii faces east towards the {{ill|Chikuma River|ja|千曲川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikuma River|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 信濃川|1=Shinano River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q734455}}, which historically marked the rear of the shrine. The traditional approach was from the west, via the mountains and the ancient Hachioji-san Manji Pass. The shrine once served the worship needs of the three former villages of Wakamiya, Kurohiko, and Shibahara. Of these, Kurohiko Village, with a population of 1,000 non-agricultural households, was reportedly devastated and dispersed by frequent floods during the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period|1=Sengoku Period (Japan)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q117415390}}. The names of Kurohiko and Wakamiya are thought to derive from the deeds of the two princes linked to the shrine's origins. == References == == Bibliography == * ''Shrines and Temples in the Shinano Cultural Series,'' 1981, {{ill|Shinano Mainichi Shimbun|ja|信濃毎日新聞社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Mainichi Shimbun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 信濃毎日新聞|1=Shinano Mainichi Shimbun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11386181}}. * ''{{ill|Kadokawa Japan Place names Dictionary|ja|角川日本地名大辞典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kadokawa Japan Place names Dictionary|1=Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4093011}} Vol. 20: Nagano Prefecture,'' 1990, [[:en:Kadokawa Shoten|Kadokawa Shoten]]. {{Commonscat}} {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub-Nagano Prefecture}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|佐良志奈神社|version=104633418|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shinano Province]] }} [[ja:佐良志奈神社]] [[Category:Buildings in Chikuma]] [[Category:History of Chikuma]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Nagano Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Sarashina-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q105849251}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

{{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|気長足姫尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Jingū|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神功皇后|1=Empress Jingū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q232803}}
{{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|大鷦鷯命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Nintoku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 仁徳天皇|1=Nintoku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q313119}} | founded = | main_hall= | other_name = Wakamiya Hachiman | main_festival = September 23 | events = | prefecture= {{ill|Chikuma|WD=Q179757}} }} '''Sarashina Shrine''' (さらしなじんじゃ) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Chikuma, Nagano|Chikuma]], [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, and under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]], it held the status of a ''gōsha'' (village shrine). == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto|ja|誉田別尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 応神天皇|1=Ōjin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q317997}} * {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|気長足姫尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Jingū|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神功皇后|1=Empress Jingū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q232803}} * {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|大鷦鷯命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Nintoku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 仁徳天皇|1=Nintoku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q313119}} == History == According to shrine tradition, it was initially enshrined by {{ill|Prince Sakaibe no Kurohiko|ja|坂合黒彦皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Sakaibe no Kurohiko|1=Prince Sakai no Kurohiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11429115}} on a ridge of the Sarashina mountains. However, following a landslide caused by an earthquake in the third year of the [[:en:Ninna|Ninna era]] (887 CE), the shrine was relocated to the mountain's base. The shrine is referred to as "Wakamiya" in the [[:en:The Tale of the Heike|Heike Monogatari]], specifically in relation to an event in the first year of the [[:en:Yōwa|Yōwa era]] when {{ill|Kiso Yoshinaka|ja|木曽義仲|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiso Yoshinaka|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 源義仲|1=Minamoto no Yoshinaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1139202}} reportedly "prostrated himself before Hachiman Shrine." It was known as Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine until the [[:en:Hōreki|Hōreki era]] (1757 CE), when it assumed its current name. By 1759, records of the [[:en:Matsushiro Domain|Matsushiro Domain]] noted the shrine’s landholdings at three ''koku''. Before the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu bunri|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神仏分離}} (separation of Shinto and Buddhism), the shrine was associated with a [[:en:Shingon Buddhism|Shingon sect temple]] known as Wakamiya Hachiman Jingū-ji. File:Sarashina-jinja torii.jpg|Torii File:Sarashina-jinja 2.jpg|Main shrine building File:Sarashina-jinja cenotaph.jpg|{{ill|Cenotaph for loyal souls|ja|忠魂碑|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cenotaph for loyal souls|qq=}} * '''Treasure Tower (Hōkyōintō):''' The grounds contain an ancient stone pagoda inscribed with the year {{ill|Eiwa era|ja|永和|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eiwa era|1=Eiwa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11131293}} 2 (1378 CE). It is believed to mark the burial site of 45 local warriors who pledged their loyalty to Prince [[:en:Prince Munenaga|Morinaga]] and prepared for death before heading to battle. It was designated a tangible cultural property of the city in 1988. * '''Earthworks:''' The northern and eastern sides of the shrine grounds retain traces of medieval fortifications, possibly linked to a {{ill|Southern Court (Japan)|ja|南朝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Southern Court (Japan)|1=Southern Dynasties|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1206988}} loyalist stronghold. * '''Slender Bronze Sword:''' Discovered during the Edo period in the nearby {{ill|Yazuka Ruins|ja|箭塚遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yazuka Ruins|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, this sword became a sacred treasure of the shrine. It drew attention from historians for demonstrating that bronze cultures from [[:en:Northern Kyushu|Northern Kyushu]] extended far beyond the {{ill|Kansai region|ja|近畿圏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kansai region|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 京阪神|1=Keihanshin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1149484}}. It was designated a tangible cultural property of the city in 1988. * '''Torii Gate:''' The current torii faces east towards the {{ill|Chikuma River|ja|千曲川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikuma River|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 信濃川|1=Shinano River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q734455}}, which historically marked the rear of the shrine. The traditional approach was from the west, via the mountains and the ancient Hachioji-san Manji Pass. The shrine once served the worship needs of the three former villages of Wakamiya, Kurohiko, and Shibahara. Of these, Kurohiko Village, with a population of 1,000 non-agricultural households, was reportedly devastated and dispersed by frequent floods during the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period|1=Sengoku Period (Japan)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q117415390}}. The names of Kurohiko and Wakamiya are thought to derive from the deeds of the two princes linked to the shrine's origins. == References == == Bibliography == * ''Shrines and Temples in the Shinano Cultural Series,'' 1981, {{ill|Shinano Mainichi Shimbun|ja|信濃毎日新聞社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Mainichi Shimbun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 信濃毎日新聞|1=Shinano Mainichi Shimbun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11386181}}. * ''{{ill|Kadokawa Japan Place names Dictionary|ja|角川日本地名大辞典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kadokawa Japan Place names Dictionary|1=Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4093011}} Vol. 20: Nagano Prefecture,'' 1990, [[:en:Kadokawa Shoten|Kadokawa Shoten]]. {{Commonscat}} {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub-Nagano Prefecture}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|佐良志奈神社|version=104633418|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:佐良志奈神社]] {{Commons category|Sarashina-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q105849251}} [[Category:Buildings in Chikuma]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:History of Chikuma]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shinano Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

File:Sarashina-jinja torii.jpg|Torii File:Sarashina-jinja 2.jpg|Main shrine building File:Sarashina-jinja cenotaph.jpg|{{ill|Cenotaph for loyal souls|ja|忠魂碑|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cenotaph for loyal souls|qq=}} * '''Treasure Tower (Hōkyōintō):''' The grounds contain an ancient stone pagoda inscribed with the year {{ill|Eiwa era|ja|永和|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eiwa era|1=Eiwa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11131293}} 2 (1378 CE). It is believed to mark the burial site of 45 local warriors who pledged their loyalty to Prince [[:en:Prince Munenaga|Morinaga]] and prepared for death before heading to battle. It was designated a tangible cultural property of the city in 1988. * '''Earthworks:''' The northern and eastern sides of the shrine grounds retain traces of medieval fortifications, possibly linked to a {{ill|Southern Court (Japan)|ja|南朝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Southern Court (Japan)|1=Southern Dynasties|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1206988}} loyalist stronghold. * '''Slender Bronze Sword:''' Discovered during the Edo period in the nearby {{ill|Yazuka Ruins|ja|箭塚遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yazuka Ruins|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, this sword became a sacred treasure of the shrine. It drew attention from historians for demonstrating that bronze cultures from [[:en:Northern Kyushu|Northern Kyushu]] extended far beyond the {{ill|Kansai region|ja|近畿圏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kansai region|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 京阪神|1=Keihanshin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1149484}}. It was designated a tangible cultural property of the city in 1988. * '''Torii Gate:''' The current torii faces east towards the {{ill|Chikuma River|ja|千曲川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikuma River|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 信濃川|1=Shinano River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q734455}}, which historically marked the rear of the shrine. The traditional approach was from the west, via the mountains and the ancient Hachioji-san Manji Pass. The shrine once served the worship needs of the three former villages of Wakamiya, Kurohiko, and Shibahara. Of these, Kurohiko Village, with a population of 1,000 non-agricultural households, was reportedly devastated and dispersed by frequent floods during the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period|1=Sengoku Period (Japan)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q117415390}}. The names of Kurohiko and Wakamiya are thought to derive from the deeds of the two princes linked to the shrine's origins. == References == == Bibliography == * ''Shrines and Temples in the Shinano Cultural Series,'' 1981, {{ill|Shinano Mainichi Shimbun|ja|信濃毎日新聞社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Mainichi Shimbun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 信濃毎日新聞|1=Shinano Mainichi Shimbun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11386181}}. * ''{{ill|Kadokawa Japan Place names Dictionary|ja|角川日本地名大辞典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kadokawa Japan Place names Dictionary|1=Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4093011}} Vol. 20: Nagano Prefecture,'' 1990, [[:en:Kadokawa Shoten|Kadokawa Shoten]]. {{Commonscat}} {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub-Nagano Prefecture}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|佐良志奈神社|version=104633418|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:佐良志奈神社]] {{Commons category|Sarashina-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q105849251}} [[Category:Buildings in Chikuma]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:History of Chikuma]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shinano Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

[[commons:Category:Sarashina-jinja]][[ja:佐良志奈神社]] [[commons:Category:Sarashina-jinja]][[ja:佐良志奈神社]] [[sn:commons:Category:Sarashina-jinja]][[ja:佐良志奈神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q105849251]] Wikidata name: Sarashina-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:06, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sarashina Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Sarashina-jinja 1.jpg | location = [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]], [[:en:Chikuma, Nagano|Chikuma]], Wakamiya 2 | coordinates = {{Coord|36.487255|N|138.1418964|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deities = {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto|ja|誉田別尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 応神天皇|1=Ōjin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q317997}}
{{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|気長足姫尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Jingū|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神功皇后|1=Empress Jingū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q232803}}
{{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|大鷦鷯命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Nintoku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 仁徳天皇|1=Nintoku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q313119}} | founded = | main_hall= | other_name = Wakamiya Hachiman | main_festival = September 23 | events = | prefecture= {{ill|Chikuma|WD=Q179757}} }} '''Sarashina Shrine''' (さらしなじんじゃ) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Chikuma, Nagano|Chikuma]], [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, and under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]], it held the status of a ''gōsha'' (village shrine). == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto|ja|誉田別尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 応神天皇|1=Ōjin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q317997}} * {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|気長足姫尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Jingū|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神功皇后|1=Empress Jingū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q232803}} * {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|大鷦鷯命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Nintoku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 仁徳天皇|1=Nintoku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q313119}} == History == According to shrine tradition, it was initially enshrined by {{ill|Prince Sakaibe no Kurohiko|ja|坂合黒彦皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Sakaibe no Kurohiko|1=Prince Sakai no Kurohiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11429115}} on a ridge of the Sarashina mountains. However, following a landslide caused by an earthquake in the third year of the [[:en:Ninna|Ninna era]] (887 CE), the shrine was relocated to the mountain's base. The shrine is referred to as "Wakamiya" in the [[:en:The Tale of the Heike|Heike Monogatari]], specifically in relation to an event in the first year of the [[:en:Yōwa|Yōwa era]] when {{ill|Kiso Yoshinaka|ja|木曽義仲|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiso Yoshinaka|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 源義仲|1=Minamoto no Yoshinaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1139202}} reportedly "prostrated himself before Hachiman Shrine." It was known as Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine until the [[:en:Hōreki|Hōreki era]] (1757 CE), when it assumed its current name. By 1759, records of the [[:en:Matsushiro Domain|Matsushiro Domain]] noted the shrine’s landholdings at three ''koku''. Before the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu bunri|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神仏分離}} (separation of Shinto and Buddhism), the shrine was associated with a [[:en:Shingon Buddhism|Shingon sect temple]] known as Wakamiya Hachiman Jingū-ji. File:Sarashina-jinja torii.jpg|Torii File:Sarashina-jinja 2.jpg|Main shrine building File:Sarashina-jinja cenotaph.jpg|{{ill|Cenotaph for loyal souls|ja|忠魂碑|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cenotaph for loyal souls|qq=}} * '''Treasure Tower (Hōkyōintō):''' The grounds contain an ancient stone pagoda inscribed with the year {{ill|Eiwa era|ja|永和|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eiwa era|1=Eiwa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11131293}} 2 (1378 CE). It is believed to mark the burial site of 45 local warriors who pledged their loyalty to Prince [[:en:Prince Munenaga|Morinaga]] and prepared for death before heading to battle. It was designated a tangible cultural property of the city in 1988. * '''Earthworks:''' The northern and eastern sides of the shrine grounds retain traces of medieval fortifications, possibly linked to a {{ill|Southern Court (Japan)|ja|南朝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Southern Court (Japan)|1=Southern Dynasties|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1206988}} loyalist stronghold. * '''Slender Bronze Sword:''' Discovered during the Edo period in the nearby {{ill|Yazuka Ruins|ja|箭塚遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yazuka Ruins|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, this sword became a sacred treasure of the shrine. It drew attention from historians for demonstrating that bronze cultures from [[:en:Northern Kyushu|Northern Kyushu]] extended far beyond the {{ill|Kansai region|ja|近畿圏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kansai region|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 京阪神|1=Keihanshin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1149484}}. It was designated a tangible cultural property of the city in 1988. * '''Torii Gate:''' The current torii faces east towards the {{ill|Chikuma River|ja|千曲川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikuma River|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 信濃川|1=Shinano River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q734455}}, which historically marked the rear of the shrine. The traditional approach was from the west, via the mountains and the ancient Hachioji-san Manji Pass. The shrine once served the worship needs of the three former villages of Wakamiya, Kurohiko, and Shibahara. Of these, Kurohiko Village, with a population of 1,000 non-agricultural households, was reportedly devastated and dispersed by frequent floods during the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period|1=Sengoku Period (Japan)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q117415390}}. The names of Kurohiko and Wakamiya are thought to derive from the deeds of the two princes linked to the shrine's origins. == References == == Bibliography == * ''Shrines and Temples in the Shinano Cultural Series,'' 1981, {{ill|Shinano Mainichi Shimbun|ja|信濃毎日新聞社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Mainichi Shimbun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 信濃毎日新聞|1=Shinano Mainichi Shimbun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11386181}}. * ''{{ill|Kadokawa Japan Place names Dictionary|ja|角川日本地名大辞典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kadokawa Japan Place names Dictionary|1=Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4093011}} Vol. 20: Nagano Prefecture,'' 1990, [[:en:Kadokawa Shoten|Kadokawa Shoten]]. {{Commonscat}} {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub-Nagano Prefecture}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|佐良志奈神社|version=104633418|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:佐良志奈神社]] {{Commons category|Sarashina-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q105849251}} [[Category:Buildings in Chikuma]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:History of Chikuma]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shinano Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

[[co:commons:Category:Sarashina-jinja]][[ja:佐良志奈神社]] [[co:commons:Category:Sarashina-jinja]][[ja:佐良志奈神社]] [[sn:commons:Category:Sarashina-jinja]][[ja:佐良志奈神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q105849251]] Wikidata name: Sarashina-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:06, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sarashina Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Sarashina-jinja 1.jpg | location = [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]], [[:en:Chikuma, Nagano|Chikuma]], Wakamiya 2 | coordinates = {{Coord|36.487255|N|138.1418964|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deities = {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto|ja|誉田別尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 応神天皇|1=Ōjin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q317997}}
{{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|気長足姫尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Jingū|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神功皇后|1=Empress Jingū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q232803}}
{{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|大鷦鷯命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Nintoku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 仁徳天皇|1=Nintoku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q313119}} | founded = | main_hall= | other_name = Wakamiya Hachiman | main_festival = September 23 | events = | prefecture= {{ill|Chikuma|WD=Q179757}} }} '''Sarashina Shrine''' (さらしなじんじゃ) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Chikuma, Nagano|Chikuma]], [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, and under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]], it held the status of a ''gōsha'' (village shrine). == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto|ja|誉田別尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 応神天皇|1=Ōjin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q317997}} * {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|気長足姫尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Jingū|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神功皇后|1=Empress Jingū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q232803}} * {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|大鷦鷯命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Nintoku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 仁徳天皇|1=Nintoku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q313119}} == History == According to shrine tradition, it was initially enshrined by {{ill|Prince Sakaibe no Kurohiko|ja|坂合黒彦皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Sakaibe no Kurohiko|1=Prince Sakai no Kurohiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11429115}} on a ridge of the Sarashina mountains. However, following a landslide caused by an earthquake in the third year of the [[:en:Ninna|Ninna era]] (887 CE), the shrine was relocated to the mountain's base. The shrine is referred to as "Wakamiya" in the [[:en:The Tale of the Heike|Heike Monogatari]], specifically in relation to an event in the first year of the [[:en:Yōwa|Yōwa era]] when {{ill|Kiso Yoshinaka|ja|木曽義仲|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiso Yoshinaka|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 源義仲|1=Minamoto no Yoshinaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1139202}} reportedly "prostrated himself before Hachiman Shrine." It was known as Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine until the [[:en:Hōreki|Hōreki era]] (1757 CE), when it assumed its current name. By 1759, records of the [[:en:Matsushiro Domain|Matsushiro Domain]] noted the shrine’s landholdings at three ''koku''. Before the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu bunri|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神仏分離}} (separation of Shinto and Buddhism), the shrine was associated with a [[:en:Shingon Buddhism|Shingon sect temple]] known as Wakamiya Hachiman Jingū-ji. File:Sarashina-jinja torii.jpg|Torii File:Sarashina-jinja 2.jpg|Main shrine building File:Sarashina-jinja cenotaph.jpg|{{ill|Cenotaph for loyal souls|ja|忠魂碑|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cenotaph for loyal souls|qq=}} * '''Treasure Tower (Hōkyōintō):''' The grounds contain an ancient stone pagoda inscribed with the year {{ill|Eiwa era|ja|永和|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eiwa era|1=Eiwa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11131293}} 2 (1378 CE). It is believed to mark the burial site of 45 local warriors who pledged their loyalty to Prince [[:en:Prince Munenaga|Morinaga]] and prepared for death before heading to battle. It was designated a tangible cultural property of the city in 1988. * '''Earthworks:''' The northern and eastern sides of the shrine grounds retain traces of medieval fortifications, possibly linked to a {{ill|Southern Court (Japan)|ja|南朝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Southern Court (Japan)|1=Southern Dynasties|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1206988}} loyalist stronghold. * '''Slender Bronze Sword:''' Discovered during the Edo period in the nearby {{ill|Yazuka Ruins|ja|箭塚遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yazuka Ruins|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, this sword became a sacred treasure of the shrine. It drew attention from historians for demonstrating that bronze cultures from [[:en:Northern Kyushu|Northern Kyushu]] extended far beyond the {{ill|Kansai region|ja|近畿圏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kansai region|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 京阪神|1=Keihanshin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1149484}}. It was designated a tangible cultural property of the city in 1988. * '''Torii Gate:''' The current torii faces east towards the {{ill|Chikuma River|ja|千曲川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikuma River|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 信濃川|1=Shinano River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q734455}}, which historically marked the rear of the shrine. The traditional approach was from the west, via the mountains and the ancient Hachioji-san Manji Pass. The shrine once served the worship needs of the three former villages of Wakamiya, Kurohiko, and Shibahara. Of these, Kurohiko Village, with a population of 1,000 non-agricultural households, was reportedly devastated and dispersed by frequent floods during the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period|1=Sengoku Period (Japan)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q117415390}}. The names of Kurohiko and Wakamiya are thought to derive from the deeds of the two princes linked to the shrine's origins. == References == == Bibliography == * ''Shrines and Temples in the Shinano Cultural Series,'' 1981, {{ill|Shinano Mainichi Shimbun|ja|信濃毎日新聞社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Mainichi Shimbun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 信濃毎日新聞|1=Shinano Mainichi Shimbun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11386181}}. * ''{{ill|Kadokawa Japan Place names Dictionary|ja|角川日本地名大辞典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kadokawa Japan Place names Dictionary|1=Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4093011}} Vol. 20: Nagano Prefecture,'' 1990, [[:en:Kadokawa Shoten|Kadokawa Shoten]]. {{Commonscat}} {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub-Nagano Prefecture}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|佐良志奈神社|version=104633418|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:佐良志奈神社]] {{Commons category|Sarashina-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q105849251}} [[Category:Buildings in Chikuma]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:History of Chikuma]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shinano Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

{{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|気長足姫尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Jingū|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神功皇后|1=Empress Jingū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q232803}}
{{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|大鷦鷯命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Nintoku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 仁徳天皇|1=Nintoku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q313119}} | founded = | main_hall= | other_name = Wakamiya Hachiman | main_festival = September 23 | events = | prefecture= {{ill|Chikuma|WD=Q179757}} }} '''Sarashina Shrine''' (さらしなじんじゃ) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Chikuma, Nagano|en|Chikuma, Nagano|WD=Q179757|lt=Chikuma}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|WD=Q127877|lt=Nagano Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, and under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines}}, it held the status of a ''gōsha'' (village shrine). == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto|ja|誉田別尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 応神天皇|1=Ōjin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q317997}} * {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|気長足姫尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Jingū|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神功皇后|1=Empress Jingū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q232803}} * {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|大鷦鷯命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Nintoku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 仁徳天皇|1=Nintoku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q313119}} == History == According to shrine tradition, it was initially enshrined by {{ill|Prince Sakaibe no Kurohiko|ja|坂合黒彦皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Sakaibe no Kurohiko|1=Prince Sakai no Kurohiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11429115}} on a ridge of the Sarashina mountains. However, following a landslide caused by an earthquake in the third year of the {{ill|Ninna|en|Ninna|WD=Q1346317|lt=Ninna era}} (887 CE), the shrine was relocated to the mountain's base. The shrine is referred to as "Wakamiya" in the {{ill|The Tale of the Heike|en|The Tale of the Heike|WD=Q830740|lt=Heike Monogatari}}, specifically in relation to an event in the first year of the {{ill|Yōwa|en|Yōwa|WD=Q729609|lt=Yōwa era}} when {{ill|Kiso Yoshinaka|ja|木曽義仲|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiso Yoshinaka|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 源義仲|1=Minamoto no Yoshinaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1139202}} reportedly "prostrated himself before Hachiman Shrine." It was known as Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine until the {{ill|Hōreki|en|Hōreki|WD=Q1194397|lt=Hōreki era}} (1757 CE), when it assumed its current name. By 1759, records of the {{ill|Matsushiro Domain|en|Matsushiro Domain|WD=Q903575|lt=Matsushiro Domain}} noted the shrine’s landholdings at three ''koku''. Before the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu bunri|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神仏分離}} (separation of Shinto and Buddhism), the shrine was associated with a {{ill|Shingon Buddhism|en|Shingon Buddhism|WD=Q550182|lt=Shingon sect temple}} known as Wakamiya Hachiman Jingū-ji. File:Sarashina-jinja torii.jpg|Torii File:Sarashina-jinja 2.jpg|Main shrine building File:Sarashina-jinja cenotaph.jpg|{{ill|Cenotaph for loyal souls|ja|忠魂碑|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cenotaph for loyal souls|qq=}} * '''Treasure Tower (Hōkyōintō):''' The grounds contain an ancient stone pagoda inscribed with the year {{ill|Eiwa era|ja|永和|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eiwa era|1=Eiwa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11131293}} 2 (1378 CE). It is believed to mark the burial site of 45 local warriors who pledged their loyalty to Prince {{ill|Prince Munenaga|en|Prince Munenaga|WD=Q541057|lt=Morinaga}} and prepared for death before heading to battle. It was designated a tangible cultural property of the city in 1988. * '''Earthworks:''' The northern and eastern sides of the shrine grounds retain traces of medieval fortifications, possibly linked to a {{ill|Southern Court (Japan)|ja|南朝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Southern Court (Japan)|1=Southern Dynasties|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1206988}} loyalist stronghold. * '''Slender Bronze Sword:''' Discovered during the Edo period in the nearby {{ill|Yazuka Ruins|ja|箭塚遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yazuka Ruins|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, this sword became a sacred treasure of the shrine. It drew attention from historians for demonstrating that bronze cultures from {{ill|Northern Kyushu|en|Northern Kyushu|WD=Q397703|lt=Northern Kyushu}} extended far beyond the {{ill|Kansai region|ja|近畿圏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kansai region|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 京阪神|1=Keihanshin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1149484}}. It was designated a tangible cultural property of the city in 1988. * '''Torii Gate:''' The current torii faces east towards the {{ill|Chikuma River|ja|千曲川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikuma River|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 信濃川|1=Shinano River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q734455}}, which historically marked the rear of the shrine. The traditional approach was from the west, via the mountains and the ancient Hachioji-san Manji Pass. The shrine once served the worship needs of the three former villages of Wakamiya, Kurohiko, and Shibahara. Of these, Kurohiko Village, with a population of 1,000 non-agricultural households, was reportedly devastated and dispersed by frequent floods during the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period|1=Sengoku Period (Japan)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q117415390}}. The names of Kurohiko and Wakamiya are thought to derive from the deeds of the two princes linked to the shrine's origins. == References == == Bibliography == * ''Shrines and Temples in the Shinano Cultural Series,'' 1981, {{ill|Shinano Mainichi Shimbun|ja|信濃毎日新聞社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Mainichi Shimbun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 信濃毎日新聞|1=Shinano Mainichi Shimbun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11386181}}. * ''{{ill|Kadokawa Japan Place names Dictionary|ja|角川日本地名大辞典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kadokawa Japan Place names Dictionary|1=Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4093011}} Vol. 20: Nagano Prefecture,'' 1990, {{ill|Kadokawa Shoten|en|Kadokawa Shoten|WD=Q1054765|lt=Kadokawa Shoten}}. {{Commonscat}} {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub-Nagano Prefecture}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|佐良志奈神社|version=104633418|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:佐良志奈神社]] {{Commons category|Sarashina-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q105849251}} [[Category:Buildings in Chikuma]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:History of Chikuma]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shinano Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

{{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|気長足姫尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Jingū|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神功皇后|1=Empress Jingū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q232803}}
{{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|大鷦鷯命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Nintoku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 仁徳天皇|1=Nintoku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q313119}} | founded = | main_hall= | other_name = Wakamiya Hachiman | main_festival = September 23 | events = | prefecture= {{ill|Chikuma|WD=Q179757}} }} '''Sarashina Shrine''' (さらしなじんじゃ) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Chikuma, Nagano|en|Chikuma, Nagano|WD=Q179757|lt=Chikuma}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|WD=Q127877|lt=Nagano Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, and under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines}}, it held the status of a ''gōsha'' (village shrine). == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto|ja|誉田別尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 応神天皇|1=Ōjin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q317997}} * {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|気長足姫尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Jingū|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神功皇后|1=Empress Jingū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q232803}} * {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|大鷦鷯命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Nintoku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 仁徳天皇|1=Nintoku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q313119}} == History == According to shrine tradition, it was initially enshrined by {{ill|Prince Sakaibe no Kurohiko|ja|坂合黒彦皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Sakaibe no Kurohiko|1=Prince Sakai no Kurohiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11429115}} on a ridge of the Sarashina mountains. However, following a landslide caused by an earthquake in the third year of the {{ill|Ninna|en|Ninna|WD=Q1346317|lt=Ninna era}} (887 CE), the shrine was relocated to the mountain's base. The shrine is referred to as "Wakamiya" in the {{ill|The Tale of the Heike|en|The Tale of the Heike|WD=Q830740|lt=Heike Monogatari}}, specifically in relation to an event in the first year of the {{ill|Yōwa|en|Yōwa|WD=Q729609|lt=Yōwa era}} when {{ill|Kiso Yoshinaka|ja|木曽義仲|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiso Yoshinaka|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 源義仲|1=Minamoto no Yoshinaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1139202}} reportedly "prostrated himself before Hachiman Shrine." It was known as Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine until the {{ill|Hōreki|en|Hōreki|WD=Q1194397|lt=Hōreki era}} (1757 CE), when it assumed its current name. By 1759, records of the {{ill|Matsushiro Domain|en|Matsushiro Domain|WD=Q903575|lt=Matsushiro Domain}} noted the shrine’s landholdings at three ''koku''. Before the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu bunri|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神仏分離}} (separation of Shinto and Buddhism), the shrine was associated with a {{ill|Shingon Buddhism|en|Shingon Buddhism|WD=Q550182|lt=Shingon sect temple}} known as Wakamiya Hachiman Jingū-ji. File:Sarashina-jinja torii.jpg|Torii File:Sarashina-jinja 2.jpg|Main shrine building File:Sarashina-jinja cenotaph.jpg|{{ill|Cenotaph for loyal souls|ja|忠魂碑|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cenotaph for loyal souls|qq=}} * '''Treasure Tower (Hōkyōintō):''' The grounds contain an ancient stone pagoda inscribed with the year {{ill|Eiwa era|ja|永和|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eiwa era|1=Eiwa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11131293}} 2 (1378 CE). It is believed to mark the burial site of 45 local warriors who pledged their loyalty to Prince {{ill|Prince Munenaga|en|Prince Munenaga|WD=Q541057|lt=Morinaga}} and prepared for death before heading to battle. It was designated a tangible cultural property of the city in 1988. * '''Earthworks:''' The northern and eastern sides of the shrine grounds retain traces of medieval fortifications, possibly linked to a {{ill|Southern Court (Japan)|ja|南朝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Southern Court (Japan)|1=Southern Dynasties|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1206988}} loyalist stronghold. * '''Slender Bronze Sword:''' Discovered during the Edo period in the nearby {{ill|Yazuka Ruins|ja|箭塚遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yazuka Ruins|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, this sword became a sacred treasure of the shrine. It drew attention from historians for demonstrating that bronze cultures from {{ill|Northern Kyushu|en|Northern Kyushu|WD=Q397703|lt=Northern Kyushu}} extended far beyond the {{ill|Kansai region|ja|近畿圏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kansai region|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 京阪神|1=Keihanshin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1149484}}. It was designated a tangible cultural property of the city in 1988. * '''Torii Gate:''' The current torii faces east towards the {{ill|Chikuma River|ja|千曲川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikuma River|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 信濃川|1=Shinano River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q734455}}, which historically marked the rear of the shrine. The traditional approach was from the west, via the mountains and the ancient Hachioji-san Manji Pass. The shrine once served the worship needs of the three former villages of Wakamiya, Kurohiko, and Shibahara. Of these, Kurohiko Village, with a population of 1,000 non-agricultural households, was reportedly devastated and dispersed by frequent floods during the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period|1=Sengoku Period (Japan)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q117415390}}. The names of Kurohiko and Wakamiya are thought to derive from the deeds of the two princes linked to the shrine's origins. == References == == Bibliography == * ''Shrines and Temples in the Shinano Cultural Series,'' 1981, {{ill|Shinano Mainichi Shimbun|ja|信濃毎日新聞社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Mainichi Shimbun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 信濃毎日新聞|1=Shinano Mainichi Shimbun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11386181}}. * ''{{ill|Kadokawa Japan Place names Dictionary|ja|角川日本地名大辞典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kadokawa Japan Place names Dictionary|1=Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4093011}} Vol. 20: Nagano Prefecture,'' 1990, {{ill|Kadokawa Shoten|en|Kadokawa Shoten|WD=Q1054765|lt=Kadokawa Shoten}}. {{Commonscat}} {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub-Nagano Prefecture}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|佐良志奈神社|version=104633418|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:佐良志奈神社]] {{Commons category|Sarashina-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q105849251}} [[Category:Buildings in Chikuma]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:History of Chikuma]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shinano Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

It was known as Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine until the {{ill|Hōreki|en|Hōreki|WD=Q1194397|lt=Hōreki era}} (1757 CE), when it assumed its current name. By 1759, records of the {{ill|Matsushiro Domain|en|Matsushiro Domain|WD=Q903575|lt=Matsushiro Domain}} noted the shrine’s landholdings at three ''koku''. Before the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu bunri|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神仏分離}} (separation of Shinto and Buddhism), the shrine was associated with a {{ill|Shingon Buddhism|en|Shingon Buddhism|WD=Q550182|lt=Shingon sect temple}} known as Wakamiya Hachiman Jingū-ji. File:Sarashina-jinja torii.jpg|Torii File:Sarashina-jinja 2.jpg|Main shrine building File:Sarashina-jinja cenotaph.jpg|{{ill|Cenotaph for loyal souls|ja|忠魂碑|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cenotaph for loyal souls|qq=}} * '''Treasure Tower (Hōkyōintō):''' The grounds contain an ancient stone pagoda inscribed with the year {{ill|Eiwa era|ja|永和|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eiwa era|1=Eiwa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11131293}} 2 (1378 CE). It is believed to mark the burial site of 45 local warriors who pledged their loyalty to Prince {{ill|Prince Munenaga|en|Prince Munenaga|WD=Q541057|lt=Morinaga}} and prepared for death before heading to battle. It was designated a tangible cultural property of the city in 1988. * '''Earthworks:''' The northern and eastern sides of the shrine grounds retain traces of medieval fortifications, possibly linked to a {{ill|Southern Court (Japan)|ja|南朝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Southern Court (Japan)|1=Southern Dynasties|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1206988}} loyalist stronghold. * '''Slender Bronze Sword:''' Discovered during the Edo period in the nearby {{ill|Yazuka Ruins|ja|箭塚遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yazuka Ruins|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, this sword became a sacred treasure of the shrine. It drew attention from historians for demonstrating that bronze cultures from {{ill|Northern Kyushu|en|Northern Kyushu|WD=Q397703|lt=Northern Kyushu}} extended far beyond the {{ill|Kansai region|ja|近畿圏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kansai region|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 京阪神|1=Keihanshin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1149484}}. It was designated a tangible cultural property of the city in 1988. * '''Torii Gate:''' The current torii faces east towards the {{ill|Chikuma River|ja|千曲川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikuma River|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 信濃川|1=Shinano River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q734455}}, which historically marked the rear of the shrine. The traditional approach was from the west, via the mountains and the ancient Hachioji-san Manji Pass. The names of Kurohiko and Wakamiya are thought to derive from the deeds of the two princes linked to the shrine's origins. == References == == Bibliography == * ''Shrines and Temples in the Shinano Cultural Series,'' 1981, {{ill|Shinano Mainichi Shimbun|ja|信濃毎日新聞社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Mainichi Shimbun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 信濃毎日新聞|1=Shinano Mainichi Shimbun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11386181}}. * ''{{ill|Kadokawa Japan Place names Dictionary|ja|角川日本地名大辞典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kadokawa Japan Place names Dictionary|1=Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4093011}} Vol. 20: Nagano Prefecture,'' 1990, {{ill|Kadokawa Shoten|en|Kadokawa Shoten|WD=Q1054765|lt=Kadokawa Shoten}}. {{Commonscat}} {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub-Nagano Prefecture}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|佐良志奈神社|version=104633418|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:佐良志奈神社]] {{Commons category|Sarashina-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q105849251}} [[Category:Buildings in Chikuma]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:History of Chikuma]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shinano Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Shikinai Shosha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]]
